name: Office of Student Leadership and Engagement
description:
The Office of Student Leadership and Engagement has been designed to enhance the co-curricular life of the Hofstra community and help students become more involved with campus events. We encourage students to participate in the many clubs, organizations and campus events that Hofstra has to offer. It is our job to see that each and every student gets the most out of their college experience. We feel very strongly that the best way to become a well-rounded student is to get involved!

Since the Office of Student Leadership and Engagement is based on this belief, we have done our best to make sure that there is something for everyone. There are hundreds of conferences, lectures, workshops, cultural programs, seminars, concerts, dance parties and socials that take place at Hofstra each semester. In addition, there are more than a hundred clubs and organizations on campus. With all that Hofstra offers, all you need is the motivation and desire to want to get involved. Through your involvement you will not only enjoy your college experience but you will develop invaluable leadership, organizational and communication skills.
summary: The Office of Student Leadership and Engagement has been designed to enhance the co-curricular life of the Hofstra community and help students become more involved with campus events.

name: Accounting Society
description: Have you ever wondered what Accountants do? Want to learn how to ace an interview? Then Accounting Society is the perfect organization for you! Our members have unique opportunities to learn about the industry and basics of Accounting, connect with various different firms, receive the latest updates and tips pertaining to the CPA exam, and more. We also connect our members with the NYSSCPA student membership to help further expand the opportunities presented to students. Come join us at our weekly meetings during Common Hour at 1:00-2:15 in CVStarr 205 with Beta Alpha Psi and Tax Society. Please email hu.acct.society1@gmail.com to join our mailing list and stay up-to-date with all our newest meetings and events!
summary: Accounting Society is a club for all majors who want to see what accounting and business in general is all about. We meet weekly during common hour at 1:00-2:15 in CV Starr 205 with Beta Alpha Psi and Tax Society.

name: Hofstra University Anthropology Club
description: The Hofstra University Anthropology Club (HUAC) welcomes anyone interested in learning about all the Earth's peoples and cultures, from early hominids to Homo sapiens, to the four disciplines within Anthropology. The club will discuss field studies, documentaries, and projects done by anthropologists past and present, as well as host fun anthropology-themed events and fundraisers!
summary: The Anthropology Club welcomes anyone interested in learning about all the Earth's people and cultures, from early hominids to Homo sapiens. The club will discuss field studies, documentaries, and projects done by anthropologists past and present!

name: Economics Student Association
description: The Economics Club shall host game nights, some of which will be social. However, some of which shall also be academic with an emphasis on behavioral economics. The Economics Club shall hold informal lectures, talks and debates relating to salient economic issues, in a digestible straightforward manner designed to be understandable and contributed to by non-majors. The club additionally may hold more formal talks either by faculty or guest speakers. In this club, we will attempt to create a writing section where students can write about the different types of economics and current events. As we progress, we will attempt to get these writings published on the Hofstra Chronicle or such sort.
summary: The Hofstra Economics Club shall seek to unite like-minded curious students and faculty in an effort to explore, learn and hopefully enjoy a complex but rewarding study.

name: Music and Entertainment Industry Student Association
description: MEISA, short for Music and Entertainment Industry Student Association, is a team dedicated to educating everyone in the Hofstra community about the music and entertainment industry; and to helping our members find internships that will lead to careers in this business. We promote music, artists, and music events. We also provide the opportunity for all members of Hofstra community to network with professionals in the music and entertainment industry.
summary: MEISA, short Music and Entertainment Industry Student Association, is a club dedicated to everything in the music. We promote music, artists, and events. We strive to help our members find internships that will lead to careers in the music industry.

name: VERITAS: Philosophy Club
description: We utilize philosophical dialogue as a means to develop and enhance independent thinking, and also as a way to hone argumentative skills. Our topic discussions range from real world questions to more abstract concepts and ideas, and we often invite professors to join in on the conversation or lead discussions. No previous experience in philosophy is required; just a passion to ask and answer big questions!
summary: We encourage independent and critical thinking through philosophical dialogue in a safe environment.

name: Psychology Club
description:
We strive to:

• Strengthen community amongst the psychology department by providing opportunities for students to meet others with the same interests!

• Connect with the rest of Hofstra and the Long Island area by participating in campus and outreach events.

• Equip students with the resources they need to inform and prepare them for the psychology-related fields they wish to pursue.

₊✩‧₊˚౨ৎ˚₊✩‧₊

Weekly meetings every Wednesday during common hour (1:00-2:00pm) in Breslin 209 (unless otherwise posted). 

Follow us on Instagram and join our GroupMe for updates on club meetings, events, social hangouts, and much more! 

₊✩‧₊˚౨ৎ˚₊✩‧₊

Instagram: @HofstraPsychClub

Linktree: https://linktr.ee/hofstrapsychclub

GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/71383545/Qei6Vc7Q
summary: The Psychology Club is for students interested in learning more about different fields of Psychology. The club provides attendees with resources to advance their interests. All undergraduate students are welcome to attend!

name: Alpha Epsilon Phi
description:
Alpha Epsilon Phi is a national sorority, founded in 1909 by seven Jewish women, dedicated to helping women become the best they can be. We prize individuality, encouraging each member to discover and develop the talents and abilities that make her unique.

Just as we value individuality, we also pride togetherness, fostering friendships, and a sense of belonging that last a lifetime. When you're an AEPHI, you'll find there's always a sister to laugh when you laugh, to listen when you talk, to lend a hand and be there for you.
summary: Alpha Epsilon Phi was nationally founded on October 24, 1909 at Barnard College and locally founded here at Hofstra University on February 26, 1989. Our chapter’s colors are green, peach, and white with the giraffe as our mascot and pearl as our gem.

name: Hofstra Entertainment Access Television
description:
Hofstra Entertainment Access Television, or the HEAT Network for short, is a student-run television network that is broadcast to the entire Hofstra University campus on channels 32.2 and 32.3. The network consists of six main shows: Director's Cut, Hofstra Today, Sports Beat, Thursday Nite Live, Spin the Wheel, and Up All Night.

The HEAT Network relies on student participation in content creation to thrive. Without our members, we would not have the exceptional variety of content that we produce each year. Each year, we continue to look towards furthering our student engagement, shows, production programs, club-to-club cooperation, and university relations.

Visit (Site Coming Soon) for more information about all of our shows, to learn how to get involved, and to watch our shows.
summary: Hofstra Entertainment Access Television is Hofstra's student-run television network. We produce six shows: Director's Cut, Hofstra Today, Sports Beat, Thursday Nite Live, Spin the Wheel, and Up All Night. Follow us on IG @heatnetwork to get involved!

name: Caribbean Student Association
description:
The Caribbean Student Association was founded upon the principle that our cultures are beautiful, but soulful when blended together. As representatives in our diverse environment, we aim to bind cultures and create unity while educating, enlightening and exciting new and current members. We try to bring a piece of home to Hofstra. 

This organization is open to all students without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, national or ethnic origin, physical or mental disability, marital or veteran status or any other characteristic protected by state or federal laws.
summary: The prominent group of CSA, formerly ACS, was founded upon the principle of acceptance of all ethnic backgrounds. As representatives in our diverse environment, we aim to bind cultures and create unity while educating,  enlightening and exciting.

name: Black Student Union
description:
The Black Student Union of Hofstra University strives to 

1. Provide a safe place where students of color can express their views concerning current events, academics, cultural arts and campus life.

2. Foster student growth, development through diversity, academics and community services and outreach.

3. Replace barriers of stereotypes, and miscommunications with education, empowerment and pride.

 

This organization is open to all students without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, national or ethnic origin, physical or mental disability, marital or veteran status or any other characteristic protected by state or federal laws.
summary: BSU (formally known as African Peoples Organization) has been in existence for over 35 years. Born in the 1970s, BSU served as an conglomerate of all minority organizations whose purpose was to attend to the needs of the minority population at Hofstra.

name: Hofstra Hellenic Society
description:
The Hofstra University Hellenic Society aims to bring together Greek and non-Greek students through cultural events, networking opportunities, and fundraising events within the Hofstra community and within the Intercollegiate Hellenic Society, a national organization founded at Hofstra University in 1980. This club seeks to keep the Greek culture alive at Hofstra by engaging students in a number of events that are both fun and educational. 

This organization is open to all students without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, national or ethnic origin, physical or mental disability, marital or veteran status or any other characteristic protected by state or federal laws.
summary: To educate, promote, and discuss Greek culture with Hofstra students regardless of religious background or nationality, and to bring together students with common interests and a desire to learn about Hellenism.

name: National Association for the Advancement of Colored People
description: The intent of the NAACP Chapter at Hofstra University is to educate, uplift, and unify the students of Hofstra University and the surrounding community. Through various programs, service projects, and positive affairs, we aim to equip students with the necessary skills to survive and flourish as young professionals, as well as develop into effective and positive leaders.
summary: The intent of the NAACP Chapter at Hofstra University is to educate, uplift, and unify the students of Hofstra University and the surrounding community.

name: The Lavender Grove
description:
The Lavender Grove is an organization powered by young adults uniting people and organizations with the LGBTQIA+ movement. We encourage the self-expression of LGBTQIA+ individuals and their allies through the provision of safe spaces and campus events. Join us for both educational and social meetings all about the LGBTQIA+ community at Hofstra!

 

This organization is open to all students without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, national or ethnic origin, physical or mental disability, marital or veteran status or any other characteristic protected by state or federal laws.
summary: The Lavender Grove is an organization powered by young adults uniting people and organizations with the LGBTQIA+ movement. We encourage the self-expression of LGBTQIA+ individuals and their allies through the provision of safe spaces and campus events.

name: Zarb Asian Pacific Students Association
description: Zarb Asian Pacific Students Association is a member of the Student Advisory Board of Zarb School of Business. We are a Zarb sponsored non-profit student organization to develop and foster relationships among Asian Pacific students. We represent and serve the interests of Asian students through sponsoring networking, business, and career events. We promote the awareness of the diverse cultures of Asia.
summary: (Graduate Students Only) We are a Zarb sponsored non-profit student organization to develop and foster relationships among Asian Pacific students. Formerly known as the Zarb Asian Student Association.

name: Danceworks Productions
description:
Danceworks Productions is a student-run organization that provides talented Hofstra students with the opportunity to perform two major performances in John Cranford Adams Playhouse per year. Its purpose is to promote the art of dance on the Hofstra University campus and to provide an opportunity for students to participate in the teaching, learning, and choreography of dance.

 

CLICK HERE to watch videos of our previous Showcases!
summary: Danceworks Productions is a student-run organization that provides talented Hofstra students with the opportunity to perform two major performances in John Cranford Adams Playhouse per year. Its purpose is to promote the art of dance on Hofstra's campus.

name: Hofstra Filmmakers' Club
description:
The Hofstra Filmmakers' Club (HFC) provides a creative and educative film community for students of all majors to participate in that offers connections with those who share a love and passion for film, creates networking opportunities for those active participants, and promotes a welcoming and collaborative environment for film enthusiasts and filmmakers, alike.

NO EXPERIENCE NECESSARY! Not sure how to do something? Don’t worry, we’ll teach you! HFC is here to help filmmakers learn and grow, allowing us all to learn great things from one another!

The Hofstra Filmmakers' Club is a place for everyone to try their hand in all areas of production, from scriptwriting to sound design, and all different types of filmmaking, while also providing a great scenario for film lovers to learn more about the craft and express their love and passion for the subject.

Come make some movie magic with us! We can't wait to meet you!
summary: The Hofstra Filmmakers' Club (HFC) is a student-run club open to all students who share a love and passion for cinema.

name: Improfstra
description:
The mission of Improfstra is to bring improvisational performance to the Hofstra community in order to foster community and a campus-wide appreciation for the art.

Ambiguity Improv provides a safe and friendly learning & performing environment for those who are interested in improvisational comedy. With a different focus each week, the Ambiguity community joins to improvise together, in addition to various performance and non-performance events throughout the year. 

The purpose of Tequila Mockingbird, Press the Button, and Factory at Lunchtime are to showcase levels of various specific forms of improv to the greater Hofstra community through performances and collaborations. PTB and Tequila perform long form improvisation, while Factory at Lunchtime works with musical improv.
summary: Improfstra hosts improv groups Ambiguity, Tequila Mockingbird, Factory at Lunchtime, and Press the Button to build community and skill in improv at Hofstra.

name: Makin' Treble
description: We are a group of talented, kind, and supportive people that love to sing, have fun, and share in the community that making music brings. We take pride in being Hofstra's only all treble-voiced a cappella group. We sing a variety of music ranging from Queen to Bonnie Tyler to Corinne Bailey Rae to Ariana Grande, all arranged by current members or alumni.
summary: Hofstra's only all treble-voiced a cappella group.

name: Masquerade Musical Theatre Co.
description:
This organization provides Hofstra students of any major with a creative outlet in all aspects of musical theatre through student-produced, directed, designed, and performed productions while exposing members to the professional musical theatre world.

In the Fall Semester, we produce a Mainstage Musical. This is a full-length musical production completely student-run. Past shows include Godspell,  American Idiot, Spelling Bee, and Company. All club members are encouraged to audition and/or to volunteer in the various different aspects of putting a full-length musical up on stage.

In the Spring Semester, we produce a Musical Revue. The Musical Revue is an hour and a half long performance of musical theatre numbers linked together by a common theme or storyline. All members of the club are encouraged to audition or volunteer in the various different aspects of producing a show.

On top of these two major productions, throughout the year, Masquerade Musical Theatre Company hosts a number of different events and master classes to help enrich the Hofstra Musical Theatre Community.
summary: Masquerade Musical Theatre Company is dedicated to providing the Hofstra community with a creative outlet in order to explore all aspects of Musical Theatre.

name: Sigma'cappella
description:
Founded as a casual singing group back in 1998, Sigma'cappella comes from humble beginnings at Hofstra University. Flash forward 25 years to today, and even though we've evolved and grown, we've largely stayed true to our roots: we're still a tight-knit group of singers who live, breathe, and thrive on music. Together, the members of Sigma'cappella belt, riff, and harmonize through a wide variety of music. Performing everything from Radiohead to Bonnie Raitt to Ariana Grande to Harry Styles and Taylor Swift (and everything in between). Sigma'cappella  competes in the Northeast region and are four-time award winning competitors in the ICCA. Most recently, they took home fourth place in their 2023 ICCA quarterfinal in Manhattan, where they also received the award for Outstanding Arrangement for "Various Storms and Saints" (arr. Jack Goodman)! We released our first studio single "Creep" in 2018, our second single "Him" in 2020, as well as our debut EP "Unveiled", and "Unveiled" (Deluxe) available on all major music streaming platforms! Check our Spotify for some new releases to come soon!

All inquiries and requests should be directed to sigmacappella@gmail.com.
summary: Hofstra's longest-running co-ed a cappella group, dating back to 1998. Winners of multiple ICCA distinctions. A close family of singers, who love each other as much as they love acappella (if not more).

name: Spectrum Players
description: We provide students with an opportunity to participate in all aspects of theatre while providing an alternative artistic outlet. We produce a mainstage production, a children's show, and an experimental works night.
summary: We provide students with an opportunity to participate in all aspects of theatre while providing an alternative artistic outlet. We produce a mainstage production, a children's show, and multiple experimental works nights.

name: Strictly Steppin'
description:
Strictly Steppin' was founded in 2000 by a diverse group of students who wanted to bring a unique form of showmanship to the Hofstra community. Originally known as "Emanon," the team practiced the African-rooted art form known as "stepping." Stepping is a compilation of claps and stomps which create beats attached to emotional expression and meaning. Strictly Steppin' prides themselves on their unity and teamwork despite variations in culture and background. The group is well known by several other teams throughout the state, and has been successful in competitions over the years. Members are always enthused to welcome interested students who are driven, creative, and committed.

 

Follow us on IG (we follow back)!: @strictlysteppinhu
summary: Hofstra University's One & Only Step Team!

name: The Hofbeats
description: The Hofbeats are one of the only co-ed a cappella groups here at Hofstra, but we're also a family that shares a love of singing and making music! We perform at many on-campus events such as Admitted Students Days, Open Houses, and tons of other opportunities that arise. Aside from performing at on-campus events, we travel to different universities in the tri-state area to perform at Invitationals with other A Cappella groups! We also compete in the International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella (ICCAs....yes, just like Pitch Perfect!) every spring. In 2018, our group won the ICCA quarterfinal at Monmouth University, and we performed at The Kimmel Center In Philadelphia for the semi-finals. In the same year, we were also awarded as Hofstra’s Performance Organization of The Year! Also, in 2021, our group placed 2nd in the ICCA Quarterfinal Round then placed 3rd in the ICCA Semifinal Round and advanced as far as the ICCA Wildcard Round - the highest placing of any Hofstra A Cappella group ever! We were also awarded Hofstra's Performance Organization of the year in the same year! In 2022, we placed 3rd in the ICCA Northeast Quarterfinal, and won the ICCA Northeast Wildcard Round! Additionally, we recently placed 3rd in the 2022 ICCA Northeast Semifinals! We don’t just perform live, though. We love to record music and put it out to Spotify, Apple Music, and all other streaming platforms. We have released our first ever EP "Listen" in the fall of 2019, as well as our single from Summer 2020, "Bye Bye Bye" by NSYNC (and you can check the music video out on YouTube!) We have been releasing several music videos, singles, and EP's since then, so check out our YouTube for some of those Music Videos- including our award-winning video "Paint it, Black" by the Rolling Stones! Our most recent project is our EP "Focal Point" on all streaming platforms! Check out our 3rd place winning ICCA Northeast Semifinal video on YouTube! Stay tuned for more projects coming up by following us on all social media platforms! (@TheHofbeats)
summary: One of Hofstra's award-winning, co-ed a cappella groups!

name: transcenDANCE
description:
TranscenDANCE is Hofstra's non-audition dance group that aims to elevate your mind as well as your body!

We include all various styles of dance such as HIP-HOP, LYRICAL, CONTEMPORARY, JAZZ, VOGUE, BALLET, MODERN, TAP ...the list is endless. We don't limit ourselves to one genre.
 
TranscenDANCE believes that everyone who has a passion for dance should be able to dance.... in other words...we don't hold auditions to join! We do hold auditions for the pieces that we perform, but we want everyone to be able to participate as much as possible! 
 
TranscenDANCE allows the essence of all the dancers to show through movement! Everyone has a story and we definitely tell our story through the art form known as DANCE!
summary: This dance group is an all-encompassing student run dance club. Everyone is invited to participate, dance, and choreograph!

name: Hofstra University Democrats
description: The mission of this organization is to educate the Hofstra community about the core principles of the democratic belief system while fostering awareness of related current events through reliable, unbiased news sources. The Hofstra University Democrats are committed to creating an inclusive and welcoming environment where members can engage in thoughtful and respectful discussions. By promoting open dialogue and encouraging diverse perspectives, the organization aims to cultivate a space where individuals feel empowered to express their opinions without fear of negativity or judgment. Through meaningful conversations and active civic engagement, we strive to inspire informed political discourse and a stronger sense of community.
summary: The Hofstra University Democrats are committed to creating an inclusive and welcoming environment where members can engage in thoughtful and respectful discussions while learning about the democratic political process and ideals.

name: College Republicans
description: College Republicans of Hofstra University is a Political Action Committee organized to establish, promote, and defend conservative ideology. Low and effective taxes, small and accountable government, personal responsibility, strong national security, and other traditional American values are the cornerstones of the Republican Party and thus are our values. We believe that America is the greatest democratic experiment the world has ever known, and remains the most influential beacon of hope and freedom in the complex modern world and we invite anyone from any political background to come and join our discussions on important topics!
summary: An inviting forum to discuss current events and act upon the ideals that we as Americans believe in.

name: American Medical Student Association
description:
Welcome to American Medical Student Association (AMSA)! We are one of the most active and engaging pre-professional organizations at Hofstra University and we provide our members with a wealth of opportunities and resources to become successful applicants for Medical/Dental/Veterinary/PA School (with an emphasis on pre-medical). From graduate-school application bootcamps to informative talks from professionals in the healthcare field, we do our best to engage pre-health students to become well-informed and well-rounded individuals in the healthcare field! Additionally, with our pre-health mentorship program that pairs underclassmen with experienced upperclassmen, AMSA aims to create useful connections/networks between like-minded individuals aspiring to change the world in which they live in a dedicated and inspiring way!

 

   Specifically, we have our weekly meeting every Wednesday in CV Starr 210 during Common Hour (1:00 p.m. -2:15pm). For each meeting, we invite a guest speaker from various healthcare fields including admission officers from different graduate schools in the medical field. Our annual spring Application Boot Camp walks through the structure of the medical school application and covers the basics of how to write a personal essay and respond to multiple mini interviews. Every meeting is also an excellent opportunity to build up a networks of pre-health peers!

 

   Ultimately, our goal is for AMSA members to become more informed and conscientious members of society; therefore, we encourage members to volunteer their services to the local community and galvanize fellow classmates to unite for a cause and leave a healthy impact on society. 

 

   The American Medical Student Association welcomes all pre-health students interested in any healthcare field. If you are interested in joining us, or have any questions, please feel free to contact us at AMSA@pride.hofstra.edu. We usually send out an email about our meeting every Monday morning. 

 

Sincerely,
2025-2026 AMSA E-Board 

 

 

 

Additional note: If you are a pre-health student, it is strongly recommended that you maintain a solid relationship with your academic advisors and become familiar with Hofstra’s Pre-Health Advisement, which will facilitate the application process to any professional schools after your education at Hofstra.
summary: The American Medical Student Association (AMSA) welcomes all types of pre-health students! AMSA's purpose is to inform students about medical school requirements, the application process, public health awareness & volunteer opportunities.

name: Biomedical Engineering Society
description:
What is BMES???

• A club that explores the intersection of engineering, medicine, and technology to advance healthcare
• Open to ALL MAJORS!!! All students interested in biomedical innovation, medical devices, AI in medicine, and more—no prior experience required!
• Hosts hands-on experiments, interdisciplinary discussions, guest speakers, research spotlights, and more to engage with real-world biomedical challenges

 

Why Join BMES?

• Learn how engineering, chemistry, biology, medicine, physics, and more come together to solve medical challenges
• Collaborate with students from diverse backgrounds to brainstorm and develop cutting-edge biomedical solutions
• Network with professors, researchers, and industry professionals in biomedical engineering and healthcare
• Opportunity – Get advice on internships, research projects, and graduate school pathways in biomedical fields

 

Biomedical challenges cannot be solved with just one field alone—they require a combination of:

• Engineering (designing medical devices & biomechanics)
• Biology & Medicine (understanding the human body)
• Chemistry & Materials Science (creating biocompatible materials & drug delivery systems)
• Physics (imaging techniques like MRI & biomechanics)
• AI & Data Science (medical imaging analysis & predictive healthcare)

At BMES, we bring these fields together to understand and create the future of medicine!

 

How to Get Involved?

Join Our Meetings! – Wednesdays from 1-2PM in SIC 229, no prior experience needed!
Stay Connected – Follow us @hofstrabmes on Instagram and join our GroupMe!
Bring Your Ideas! – Have a cool topic or experiment in mind? Let us know, and we’ll make it happen!
summary: An academic club that meets in the bioengineering lab to explore the intersection of engineering, medicine, and technology to advance healthcare and patient quality of life!

name: Financial Management Association
description: Welcome to the Financial Management Association (FMA) at Hofstra University, the inclusive finance club where students from all majors can explore and enjoy the world of finance and business. As Hofstra's chapter of a national organization, we're committed to making finance accessible and engaging for everyone. Our meetings and events provide a broad understanding of finance-related topics, ensuring that complex concepts are broken down and discussed in a way that resonates with all members. Whether you're a finance enthusiast or completely new to the field, FMA offers dynamic discussions, interactive workshops, and networking opportunities, all aimed at expanding financial literacy while having fun. Join us to connect with peers, enhance your understanding of finance, and embark on a journey of learning and personal growth within a welcoming community.
summary: The FMA is the premier Finance Organization at Hofstra University. FMA equips students with the necessary skills to succeed in life and the financial industry. The FMA is open to all majors who want to learn about finance.

name: Hofstra American Marketing Association
description:
The Hofstra American Marketing Association (HAMA) is one of the largest undergraduate, pre-professional organizations at Hofstra University. HAMA takes pride in producing the next generation of marketers. In conjunction with the Frank G. Zarb School of Business, we offer a number of opportunities for not only marketing majors, but for all business majors. HAMA has built a strong membership base and continues to grow, not only in number, but also in the respect endowed from the business school and the university as a whole.

To become a member and obtain additional benefits, please visit ama.org

If you are interested in joining, feel free to message any of the current board members listed or email us at hofstramarketingassociation@gmail.com
summary: The Hofstra American Marketing Association (HAMA) is one of the pre-professional organizations at Hofstra University. HAMA takes pride in maintaining a welcoming environment with abundant opportunities for the next generation of business professionals.

name: Phi Alpha Delta
description:
Mission, Vision & Core Values

Mission

Phi Alpha Delta Law Fraternity, International is a professional law fraternity advancing integrity, compassion and courage through service to the student, the school, the profession and the community.

Vision

We are the preeminent law fraternity promoting the bonds of fraternalism and we are the leader in the development and advancement of professional ideals.

Core Values

Bound together by tradition and our common interest in the law, we share these core values: Integrity, Compassion, Courage, Professionalism, Service, Diversity, and Innovation.

 

Declaration of Purpose

The purpose of this Fraternity shall be to form a strong bond uniting students and teachers of the law with members of the Bench and Bar in a fraternal fellowship designed to advance the ideals of liberty and equal justice under law.
summary: The purpose of this Fraternity shall be to form a strong bond uniting students and teachers of the law with members of the Bench and Bar in a fraternal fellowship designed to advance the ideals of liberty and equal justice under law.

name: Hofstra Tax Society
description:
The Tax Society offers Hofstra students the opportunity to connect and network with local accounting firms as well as gain real-life experience through the VITA program, run in conjunction with the IRS. Students can volunteer to file tax returns for low-income residents throughout Long Island, providing them with a hands-on learning experience in communicating with clients, utilizing tax software, and a chance for community service.

*VITA participation required for membership acceptance*
summary: The Tax Society provides networking and tax experience to members.  Chiefly, we organize the VITA/TCE program, where members volunteer to prepare actual tax returns for low-income individuals.

name: Hofstra Hillel (Undergraduate Student Group)
description:
We encourage you to learn more and discover how Hillel can impact your life.

Hillel is about:`

  • Community
  • Leadership Development
  • Tzedek (justice)
  • Travel
  • Israel
  • Shabbat
  • Holiday Celebrations
  • Sports
  • Professional Development
  • Jewish Learning

We take great pride in the social, spiritual and intellectual growth that students realize during their time spent at Hofstra.  And thanks to the countless events throughout the year, students have ample opportunities to explore Judaism and connect with a friendly, diverse group of people.
summary: Hofstra Hillel, the Center for Jewish Life on Campus, is a dynamic community and one of the most active student organizations at Hofstra. Hillel provides a wide variety of exciting opportunities for students to discover, explore, and celebrate Judaism.

name: Hofstra Intervarsity Christian Fellowship
description:
InterVarsity Christian Fellowship seeks to see lives transformed, Hofstra's campus renewed and world changers developed through creating spaces to encounter and respond to God and to apply the teachings of Jesus to life at college. All are welcome!

Follow us on Instagram to stay up to date with all our events! @hofstraivcf
summary: InterVarsity Christian Fellowship seeks to see lives transformed, the campus renewed and world changers developed through creating spaces to encounter and respond to God and to apply the teachings of Jesus to life at college. All are welcome!

name: Newman Club
description:
Hofstra Newman Club is the student-run Catholic club on campus and is set out to spread the loving message of the Catholic Church to all students at Hofstra. We build our meetings and events upon the four pillars of prayer, education, service, and community in order to build a family with a common identify in their faith. All are welcome for great discussions and to learn! Follow us on Instagram @hofstra.newman

Newman Club meetings are held every Wednesday at 9:30 PM in Student Center Room 143. As for regular events, we offer various social events, theology events, prayer opportunities, and much more throughout the semesters. In addition, we offer a retreat every fall, giving the students a chance to step away from college life and grow more deeply in the faith.

Catholic Campus Ministry, which works closely with Newman Club, holds Mass every Sunday at 5 PM and Wednesday at 9 PM in the Greenhouse. We have digital men's and woman's Bible study with our FOCUS missionaries.

If you have any questions, stop by our CCM office in Student Center Room 213-D, email us at hofstranewman@gmail.com. If you ever need to contact a priest, our chaplain Father Chris Sullivan can be contacted by catholicchaplain@hofstra.edu.
summary: The Newman Club is set out to spread the Catholic faith across campus through the pillars of prayer, service, and community. Masses are held every Sunday at 5PM and Wednesday at 9PM in the Greenhouse, Club meetings follow Wednesday Mass.

name: Hofstra Concerts
description: Hofstra Concerts is a student-run organization that provides all members of the Hofstra University community with nationally known and locally emerging artists at a significantly reduced cost. Members of this club get to learn the skills needed to work in the music industry on a professional level and will get to participate in the planning of each concert from the very beginning to the end. Hofstra Concerts plans and holds regular Coffeehouse shows, Battle for Music Fest, and the annual Music Fest in the Spring.
summary: Hofstra Concerts is a student-run organization that is dedicated to bringing live music to Hofstra University, for the students, by the students, since 1971.

name: FORM Club
description:
The FORM Gallery (original name) was founded in 2008, and disappeared until 2012 when two students and a professor teamed up to bring the gallery back on the scene. 
 
Since then the student-run gallery has held dozens of student shows in Calkins Hall, giving students the opportunity to exhibit their work. Art Club welcomes every medium of art. If a student is interested in showing they must submit an application at the beginning of the semester. 
 
Members get free trips to NYC galleries and art museums, and can participate in group art activities such as pictionary, charades, paint nights, and many more.  We are always open to new ideas and broadening our horizons.
 
Membership is open to all Hofstra undergraduate students!
summary: We are Hofstra's main art club and manage the gallery spaces within Calkins.  We welcome all majors and skill levels to join us for games, activities, and various art-making activities!

name: Women's Club Soccer
description: Designed to give female identifying soccer players a competitive, intercollegiate soccer program outside of the school's D1 program. We play other college club teams throughout the Northeast, primarily in NY, NJ, CT, and PA.
summary: Designed to give female soccer players a competitive, intercollegiate soccer program outside of the school's D1 program. We play other college club teams throughout the Northeast, primarily in NY, NJ, CT, and PA.

name: Club Baseball
description: Provides students with the opportunity to participate in competitive and organized baseball into college and beyond. Competes against other institutions in the National Club Baseball Association.
summary: Provides students with the opportunity to participate in competitive and organized baseball into college and beyond. Competes against other institutions in the National Club Baseball Association.

name: Equestrian Club
description:
CONTACT US AT equestrian.club@pride.hofstra.edu

 

HUET is a member of IHSA (Intercollegiate Horse Shows Association), and competes against other schools in the region. IHSA promotes competition for horseback riders of all skill levels, who compete individually and as a team at Regional, Zone, and National levels.  HUET private lessons/practices take place weekly at Sagamore Hill Stables in Northport, New York under Coach Debbie Rittereiser. HUET participates in approximately 9 shows a year at the collegiate level.  Some of the members continue on to Regionals, Zones and even Nationals. When Joining HUET, the coaches will first evaluate prospective members and will discuss with them what is an appropriate competing level for them. This will be based off of showing history, ability, experience, and class standing. There is a show class for everyone (Who have basic riding skills) because the classes that students are eligible to compete in range from Introductory (Walk/Trot only)  to Advanced (Open fences and Open Flat). All of the riders on the team are required to come to shows (either to compete, or support the team).

In IHSA, every school hosts or co-hosts one show and the host school will provide horses and the facilities for the show. At college shows, names are drawn at complete random to determine on which horse the riders will compete. Without a warm up, Students then show in their divisions and when placing 6th or higher, they will receive individual points and point riders will receive team points for their school. When students accumulate 36 points they will then place out of their class into a higher class and qualify for regionals. IHSA is different from the typical Hunter/Equitation show, in that riders do not use a familiar horse.

We are competitive but having fun and learning always come first!
summary: HUET is a supportive group of teammates from beginners to advanced riders. We take weekly lessons at Sagamore Hill Stables and are a proud member of the IHSA (The Intercollegiate Horse Shows Association), where we compete against fellow college students.

name: Women's Club Lacrosse
description:
If you love the sport of lacrosse or you want to be apart of something special this is the club for you! We have a range of experience on the team from players who just picked up a stick to players who have been playing since they were young. We are competitive but have a lot of fun together at the same time. We also participate in fundraising events and activities in order to raise money for various organizations.

We practice twice a week: Tuesday and Thursday nights from 8:30 to 10:30 (see our GroupMe for updates on location

If you want to make friends for life come join us, you won't be disappointed!
summary:
Join Women's Club Lacrosse!
Competitive and fun, no experience required. 
Equipment can be provided if needed!
Practices are Tuesday and Thursday nights

name: Men's Club Rugby
description:
Hofstra Rugby is comprised of dedicated student athletes who come together as brothers to play the sport they love. Please do not hesitate to email or text the phone number provided we’re always looking to have new members join 
Our club dates back to 1935 and is one of the oldest in the area. With a winning tradition, Hofstra continues to recruit young men who strive for perfection on and off the field. No experience is necessary to join. We pride ourselves on teaching new players the skills they need to become the best rugby players possible.
summary: Hofstra Rugby is comprised of dedicated student athletes who strive for perfection on and off the field.No experience is necessary to join. We pride ourselves on teaching new players the skills they need to become the best rugby players possible.

name: Women's Club Rugby
description:
The Hofstra University Women's Rugby Football Club is a club sports team open to anyone who would like to play. It is a fun, exciting and high-energy sport that is a great way to meet new people and stay in shape. 

One of the great things about this sport is that there is a position for everyone: big or small, short or tall, rugby could be for you! No prior Rugby or sports experience is necessary to join the team and we are always welcoming new players to our rugby family! We are looking for anyone committed to working hard, having fun and winning games!

https://www.instagram.com/reel/C69SFhIAKJI/?igsh=cWtkZnp6b3k2b3My
summary: The Hofstra University Women's Rugby Football Club is a club sports team open to anyone who would like to play. It is a fun, exciting and high-energy sport that is a great way to meet new people and stay in shape.

name: Club Ice Hockey
description:
Hofstra Ice Hockey gives students of all skill levels a chance for a competitive college hockey experience. The team is a very close-knit group that plays at a high level together and exudes sportsmanship and school spirit. Our team is a member of the Empire AAU and is part of the Amateur Athletic Union (AAU). Hofstra Ice Hockey plays its home games and practices at the Freeport Recreational Center in Freeport, NY. Additionally, SGA puts on campus-wide programming and collaborates with clubs, organizations, and students to create positive change at Hofstra.

name: Phi Delta Theta
description:
Phi Delta Theta was built on three pillars that haven’t budged an inch since the Fraternity was founded by “The Immortal Six” way back in 1848 at Miami University. The pillars are: The cultivation of friendship among its members, the acquirement individually of a high degree of mental culture, and the attainment personally of a high standard of morality. Shortened, we refer to the principles as friendship, sound learning and rectitude.

Helping every individual to meet his true potential is the bedrock of the Phi Delta Theta Fraternity. By celebrating each person’s true self, and by learning from each other’s strengths while helping to improve each other’s weaknesses, every member of Phi Delta Theta develops into a greater version of himself than he could ever on his own. Rather than try to find young men to “mold” into some ideal, we celebrate the uniqueness of each individual and, through encouragement, values, example and brotherhood, empower every brother to exceed his personal expectations.

We are the New York Kappa Chapter of the Phi Delta Theta Fraternity.
summary: Phi Delta Theta was built on three pillars that haven’t budged an inch since the Fraternity was founded by “The Immortal Six” way back in 1848 at Miami University. These pillars are: Friendship, Sound Learning, and Moral Rectitude.

name: Residence Hall Association
description:
The Residence Hall Association exists to improve the quality of life for Hofstra residential students by offering social and educational programs based on students' interests as well as promoting a safe, supportive, and welcoming community. We host programs for our residents throughout the year on a large and small scale, some of which include the Back to School Pool Party, the Halloween Carnival, Roll With RHA, and Big Bucks Bingo!

 

In addition, we serve as a voice for students by taking on advocacy initiatives. Our Advocacy Committee sees this aspect through by polling our members and other residents to see what issues need to be addressed, researching these specific topics and solutions, setting up meetings with the appropriate Hofstra staff, and making change a reality. One success of ours includes the installment of filtered water stations on all 13th floors of each residential tower!

 

As a member of RHA, you would be involved in the planning and implementation of our programs and initiatives as well as actively serving residents with pride while striving to improve residential life on campus.
summary: RHA's mission is to serve as a voice for the residential students of Hofstra University and to enhance the educational, social, recreational, and cultural aspects of residence life while providing leadership training within the residence halls.

name: Beta Alpha Psi
description:
Summary: 

Founded in 1919, Beta Alpha Psi is an honor organization for financial information students and professionals. There are over 300 chapters on college and university campuses with over 300,000 members initiated since Beta Alpha Psi's formation. 

Objective:

The primary objective of Beta Alpha Psi is to encourage and give recognition to scholastic and professional excellence in the business information field.

This includes:

  • promoting the study and practice of accounting, finance and information systems,
  • providing opportunities for self-development, service and association among members and practicing professionals,
  • and encouraging a sense of ethical, social, and public responsibility.

Delta Pi Chapter: 

At Hofstra University, the Delta Pi chapter is focused on promoting the international goals of BAP within its members. We do so through our professional meetings and exciting events. If you wish to learn more visit, please visit our website at http://hubap108.wixsite.com/home or reach out to us at HU.BAP.108@gmail.com.
summary: Beta Alpha Psi is an honor organization for financial information students and professionals. At Hofstra University, the Delta Pi chapter is focused on promoting the international goals of BAP through our professional meetings and exciting events.

name: Zarb Women in Business
description:
(Graduate Students Only) Zarb Women in Business is a professional association at Hofstra University that strives to promote the empowerment of women and women's presence in all levels and industries in business by providing opportunities to network, attend conferences, receive career development, and participate in social events and community service.

This program is open to all students without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, national or ethnic origin, physical or mental disability, marital or veteran status or any other characteristic protected by state or federal laws.
summary: (Graduate Students Only) Zarb Women in Business is a professional association at Hofstra University that strives to promote the empowerment of women and women's presence in all levels and industries in business.

name: Hofstra Gospel Ensemble
description:
Students who join this organization do not only become part of a choir, but a family that prays together and shares testimonies and trials to uplift and support one another.  This group has been an avenue for artistic expression to some and spiritual guidance to others.  The one thing that is certain is that the sight and sound of the Hofstra Gospel Ensemble will touch your soul in some way.

The Hofstra Gospel Ensemble is a cast of musicians, singers, dancers, and ministers who are not only gifted, but spiritually rooted. This group is comprised of students and people from all over the Long Island and New York area that desire to express themselves through music while reaching the ears and hearts of people on campus and abroad. HGE is a pivotal force at Hofstra socially, artistically, and spiritually.

This group primarily participates and performs at events held by other student organizations, Hofstra University, and other local churches and venues.  The main attraction and most popular affair is a concert held at the end of each semester, entitled “Joy Night.”  This climactic show boasts performances by choirs and groups all over New York, but is mostly a platform for HGE to reach students, friends, and family through song, dance, and pure ministry.
summary: Hofstra Gospel Ensemble is a nondenominational club. A cast of musicians, singers, and ministers who are not only gifted, but spiritually rooted. This group is comprised of students and people from all over the Long Island and New York area.

name: Tau Epsilon Phi
description:
Tau Epsilon Phi’s Sigma Alpha Sigma (ΣAΣ) chapter, founded in 1969, is Hofstra University’s first fraternity, built on Friendship 🤝, Chivalry ⚔️, and Service 💙. For over five decades, we have upheld a Brotherhood committed to social justice, leadership, and philanthropy—making a real impact through community service, campus involvement, and advocacy.

 

While we may not be the largest, our influence is undeniable. From cancer walks to seizure awareness campaigns 💜, we take action where it matters. We also foster strong bonds beyond our chapter, collaborating with CCNY, Queens College, and other TEP chapters to grow together.

 

TEP is more than just a fraternity—it’s a movement 🚀. If you’re looking for a Brotherhood that stands for change, leadership, and lifelong connections, Tau Epsilon Phi Fraternity is the place to be! 🔥
summary: Tau Epsilon Phi’s Sigma Alpha Sigma (ΣAΣ) chapter, founded in 1969, is Hofstra University’s first fraternity, built on Friendship 🤝, Chivalry ⚔️, and Service 💙. We are a Brotherhood committed to social justice, leadership, and philanthropy.

Phi Sigma Sigma is a dynamic sisterhood of powerful women fostering uncompromising principles, igniting positive change, and embracing individuality. While also being dedicated to the twin ideals of promoting the brotherhood of man and alleviation of the world's pain. Founded in 1913 in New York City at Hunter College, Phi Sigma Sigma is recognized as a successful women’s philanthropic and social organization. name: Men's Club Lacrosse
description:
The Team is entering our Spring 2025 season in the NCLL with 17 playoff appearances in the last 20 years and is looking to build on our season last year with a National Championship this upcoming year. 

The National College Lacrosse League is a Men's lacrosse league comprised of mostly Eastern US college lacrosse clubs (non-varsity). There are approximately 100 teams divided into 12 conferences. The programs are split into Division I and Division II, with a championship held for each at the end of the season. Many of the clubs are at schools that currently have varsity NCAA Men's lacrosse programs.


The Hofstra University Men’s Lacrosse Club is an active sports club consisting of around 35-40 members. We play a brief Fall Ball season and a full Spring season, culminating in the NCLL playoffs during the first week of May. Most of our players were competitive high school players and either decided not to walk on to the Hofstra Varsity team or did so unsuccessfully. We are a competitive team and have made the NCLL playoffs for most seasons in recent history. Practice is usually twice a week with games most weekends in the Spring. As important as our on-the-field play is our extensive social calendar, which is not detailed here for obvious reasons.

The team is run like a NCAA level team without all the headaches. 

The team is lead by a President, Vice President, Treasurer, and Secretary. Besides leading the team, they make sure that the team is running smoothly in the background. 

Practices in the fall/spring are Monday and Wed. in the Stadium from 8:00-10:30pm. In the spring we play around 15 games. In the Fall we play about 5 games.
summary: The Team is entering our Fall 2023 season in the National Club Lacrosse League (NCLL) with 17 playoff appearances in the last 19 years and is looking to build on our season last year with a National Championship this upcoming year.

name: Alpha Kappa Psi
description:
College students everywhere are discovering that Alpha Kappa Psi is much more than just another organization or club—it is a unique, prestigious association of students, professors, graduates and professionals with common interests and goals. They join Alpha Kappa Psi to take advantage of valuable educational, friendship and networking opportunities.

Guided by five core values, Alpha Kappa Psi is an international organization that focuses on providing ethical leadership and professional development. Through educational programs, community service and a hands-on application of formal education, Alpha Kappa Psi members grow and develop practices most needed in business leadership today: Responsibility, Resilience, Self-Awareness, and Critical Thinking.

Regardless of your major, when you join Alpha Kappa Psi, you join a focused group of individuals who are committed to keeping their skills sharp and careers on track. The fraternity offers developmental programs such as the Principled Business Leadership Institute, Academy, Case Competition, College of Leadership and webinars. On the local level, members gain many valuable skills as they operate their chapter as "franchise” of Alpha Kappa Psi. Such professional development, when marketed to employers, will set you apart from your peers.

Donate to our philanthropy, the Ronald McDonald House of Long Island!
summary: Alpha Kappa Psi is Hofstra's only Professional Co-ed Business Fraternity that promotes professional development, networking, leadership, unity, and brotherhood. We are open to all majors, and invite ambitious students to join our organization.

name: Panhellenic Association of Hofstra University
description:
Hofstra Panhellenic Association
To join a Panhellenic Sorority, potential new members will go through the Formal Recruitment process during the Spring Semester. The Formal Recruitment process is a values-based, mutual selection process where potential new members will meet each of our 6 Panhellenic sororities. At the end of the Recruitment process, a potential new member may receive an invitation to join 1 sorority.
 
Recruitment will be held from Friday, February 13th, 2026 until Tuesday, February 17th, 2026. The schedule is as follows:
  • Friday, February 13th - PNM Orientation/Meet the Recruitment Counselors
  • Saturday, February 14th - Sisterhood Round, 
  • Sunday, February 15th - Philanthropy Round
  • Monday, February 16th - Preference Round
  • Tuesday, February 17th -  Bid Day
To sign up for Panhellenic sorority recruitment, follow this link: Panhellenic Sorority Recruitment Sign-Up
 
Things to Know
  • You must be a full-time, fully matriculated Hofstra University student in order to join a Greek organization.
  • You are not eligible to join a sorority unless you have a 2.5 Cumulative GPA with Hofstra University. Please also note many sororities have their own GPA requirements and use potential new member's GPA as a factor in the mutual selection process. 
  • Panhellenic Sorority Dues range from $400-$700 per semester. If you are interested in joining a Panhellenic Sorority, it is highly recommended that you have a financial plan to be able to pay your dues each semester.
  • Potential new members are encouraged to attend Meet the Greeks or other Panhellenic sponsored events. This will be the first opportunity for potential members to meet members in all of the organizations and eventually find the organization that is the best fit for them.
Contact Info: panhellenic@pride.hofstra.edu
summary: The Panhellenic Council of Hofstra University is the coordinating body of the National Panhellenic Conference sororities and one associate member sorority on campus.

name: Alpha Theta Beta Sorority
description: Alpha Theta Beta Sorority was founded on November 17th, 1936, and was the first Greek organization on Hofstra's campus. Being the first Greek Organization has given the sisters of Alpha Theta Beta the ability to build traditions, as Hofstra has built its own traditions. The sisters of Alpha Theta Beta strive for greatness inside and outside of the sorority and always live by the motto of "Sisterhood, Sincerity, and Cooperation."
summary: Alpha Theta Beta Sorority was established as the first Greek organization on Hofstra University's campus in 1936. The sisters strive for greatness inside and outside of Greek Life and uphold the motto of "Sisterhood, Sincerity, and Cooperation."

name: Nexus Yearbook
description:
The purpose of the Hofstra Nexus Yearbook is to compile, edit, and publish a yearbook for the Hofstra community. The yearbook is for undergraduate students. It binds together memories of the school year, and serves as frozen time for those graduating.

The books has several sections, including Greek Life, Student Life, Faculty acknowledgments, Club photos, Sports, and of course, graduating seniors. It is a great opportunity for students to become involved in all activities on campus as well as explore his or her own interests. 

The club is not highly demanding but requires those who possess drive, independence and responisibility.
summary: The purpose of the Hofstra Nexus Yearbook is to compile, edit, and publish a yearbook for the Hofstra community. The yearbook is for undergraduate students. It binds together memories of the school year, and serves as frozen time for those graduating.

name: Ski and Snowboard Club
description: We are the Ski and Snowboard Club at Hofstra University. The mission of this organization is to expose and educate the Hofstra Community on the arts of snowboarding and skiing. We will seek opportunities in providing members the experience of riding at different and new locations all throughout the Northeast, including ski resorts such as Okemo, Hunter Mountain, Mount Snow, and Windham. It will also be our goal to mentor students in building their character, confidence, and trust within their peers in a productive learning environment.
summary: The mission of this organization is to expose and educate the Hofstra Community on the arts of snowboarding and skiing. We will seek opportunities in providing members the experience of riding at different locations throughout the northeast.

name: Alpha Phi
description:
Alpha Phi is a sisterhood of outstanding women supporting one another in lifelong achievement.

The sisterhood of Alpha Phi stretches from coast to coast through 173 collegiate campuses and more than 200,000 members.  Sisters share a commitment to excellence and a strong desire to help one another and their communities.  Alpha Phis are leaders, scholars, contributors and lifetime members of a sisterhood that values these traits. 
  
Alpha Phi assists college women in making informed choices to reach their highest potential. Alpha Phi enhances and promotes each member's development and learning by focusing on sisterhood, service, scholarship, love, loyalty and character development. 

The innovative leadership and organizational practices of Alpha Phi's Founders in 1872 set the spirit for the Alpha Phi of today...a sisterhood that values the past but looks forward to the progress offered by the future. (from alphaphi.org)
summary:
Alpha Phi is a sisterhood of outstanding women supporting one another in lifelong achievement.
The sisterhood of Alpha Phi stretches from coast to coast through 173 collegiate campuses and more than 200,000 members.

name: Pi Kappa Alpha
description: Pi Kappa Alpha consists of Scholars, Leaders, Athletes and Gentlemen. If you exemplify one or several of these ideals, you could be just the type of person that PIKE is looking for.
summary: Pi Kappa Alpha consists of Scholars, Leaders, Athletes and Gentlemen. If you exemplify one or several of these ideals, you could be just the type of person that PIKE is looking for.

name: The Nu Tau Chapter of Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority Inc.
description:
Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority Incorporated is a historically African American sorority founded in 1922 at Butler University in Indianapolis, Indiana by seven educators. We set ourselves apart from the other sororities in many ways. One of these ways is by being the only sorority in the National Pan Hellenic Council to be founded on a different campus. Since 1922 we have expanded to host thousands of chapters all over the world including Bermuda, The Virgin Islands, and Germany. We pride ourselves in giving back to the community through various different philanthropies. Scholarship, Sisterhood, and Service are the three tenets of excellence that we are based on. We have over 80,000 members from various ethnic backgrounds. www.sgrho1922.com Is the link to our national website.

                  In 1993 nine distinguished young women attending Hofstra University were in search of a sisterhood. They sought after an organization that was a combination of esteem, class, sisterhood, and service. Not satisfied with what Hofstra had to offer at that time, they decided to broaden their search. Finally they found an organization that met and surpassed their standards. Finally, they found Sigma Gamma Rho. And on December 11, 1993 Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority Inc. Nu Tau Chapter was established. Nu Tau chapter has dedicated years of service and enrichment to the Hofstra community and is looking forward to continuing that tradition. Our goal is to continue to enrich the Hofstra Community and of course to fulfill our motto "Greater Service, Greater Progress". To see the various programs we have administered on campus and to follow our upcoming ones please visit Nu Tau Chapter Facebook Page or Instagram page @Nutausgrho or our website https://www.nutausgrho1922.com/
summary: Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority Incorporated is a historically African American sorority founded in 1922 at Butler University in Indianapolis, Indiana by seven educators. Our guiding principles are sisterhood,scholarship, and service.

name: Sigma Alpha Epsilon
description: Sigma Alpha Epsilon strives to give young men the leadership, scholarship, service and social experiences they need to excel in the walls outside their campus and once they graduate. We firmly believe membership is for life. In addition, we strive to mold our members into gentlemen so they can set an example in today's society.
summary: The mission of Sigma Alpha Epsilon is to promote the highest standards of friendship, scholarship and service for our members based upon the ideals set forth by our Founders and as specifically enunciated in “The True Gentleman.”

name: Inter-Fraternity Council
description:
The Inter-Fraternity Council, otherwise known as IFC, is an organization comprised of representation from each of the Six active social fraternities on Hofstra's campus. These fraternities include: Pi Lambda Phi, Phi Delta Theta, Pi Kappa Alpha, Phi Kappa Theta, Sigma Alpha Epsilon, and Tau Epsilon Phi. 

 

Follow us on Instagram at @hofstraifc for important news and updates.

 

The mission of our organization is to keep all fraternities informed about the upcoming events throughout the semester. The purpose of this organization is to bring all the social fraternities together under one group. Our goal is to recruit new members and better Greek Life as a whole. We provide an open forum for all people to come and share any issues or suggestions they might have. As well as announce their upcoming events and philanthropies. The E-Board is comprised of members from different organizations leading Greek Life at Hofstra to success and excellence.
summary: The Inter-Fraternity Council at Hofstra works to represent and provide a community and opportunities for our 7 organizations . Our mission is to bring members of social fraternities together to recruit new members and better the Greek Life community.

name: Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity Inc.
description: The objectives of this Fraternity shall be: to stimulate the ambition of its members; to prepare them for the greatest usefulness in the causes of humanity, freedom, and dignity of the individual;  to encourage the highest and noblest form of manhood; and to aid down-trodden humanity in its efforts to achieve higher social, economic and intellectual status.
summary: Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity Inc., develops leaders, promotes brotherhood and academic excellence, while providing advocacy and service for our community.

name: Hofstra Athletic Training Society

summary: HATS is a pre-professional graduate student organization for those interested in the profession of athletic training. HATS participates in a variety of community service, educational and social events annually.

name: Hofstra University Model United Nations
description:
Model United Nations is an organization that involves discussion about pressing international issues and learning about the role and function of the United Nations.
Students have the opportunity to attend a collegiate Model UN conference and represent countries in various UN committees in an effort to solve global problems.
Club members also serve as staff for Hofstra's annual Model UN Conference for high school students.
This club allows members to gain leadership and public speaking skills as well as meet new people with similar interests. No prior knowledge of the UN or politics is required to join the club.
summary: Model United Nations is a club for students interested in research and debating contemporary international issues in the style of the United Nations. A great club for students interested in public speaking, international affairs, and meeting others.

name: Muslim Student's Association
description:
The Muslim Student Association strives to serve Islam and the Muslims on campus by facilitating appropriate outlets for Muslim students to practice their faith and fulfill their religious obligations. 

*Office Space is Room 208A in the Student Center*

*Prayer Space is Student Center Room 201*

name: Hofstra Graduate Marketing Association
description: The Hofstra Graduate Marketing Association is open to membership for all Hofstra Frank G. Zarb Graduate Business students. The primary objective of the Marketing Association is to assist all members with the continuing challenges faced in marketing careers. In addition, this organization helps Zarb business students by providing them with opportunities to come into contact with well-established business professionals via corporate visits and networking events. As a result, Frank G. Zarb Graduate Business students that are members of this organization get a glimpse into the Marketing industry and gain an edge in the business world.
summary: (Graduate Students Only) The primary objective of the Marketing Association is to assist all members with the continuing challenges faced in marketing careers. In addition, this organization helps Zarb business students by providing them with oppurtuniti

Bringing together alumni, current students, and business professionals to create opportunities for our members and also learn more about American culture.

description:

name: Zarb Black & Hispanic M.B.A./M.S. Association
description:
Mission

To promote and encourage professional networking and leadership development among Black & Hispanic MBA students, Zarb School of Business, and business professionals.

Our Goals

  • Encourage mentoring relationships with alumni and Black and Hispanic MBA’s through:
    1. Promoting and coordinating with local chapters, like NSHMBA & NBMBA, for networking events
    2. Hosting speaker events for students to learn about topics like networking and career advancement
    3. Driving positive change by partnering with a community service organization to help mentor Black, Hispanic and underrepresented youth.

Why Should You Join

  1. We are an organization that is forward looking. We are going to be proactive and encourage member participation.
  2. We are focused on making a positive difference at Hofstra University and in being the driving force of positive change
  3. We promote and encourage diversity
  4. Conduct workshops that are focused on Leadership
  5. We will allow you the opportunity to make a difference. This association is focused on giving credit where credit is due. We want to hear your ideas and provide you with feedback.
  6. Networking will be a key focus this semester. We would like to provide members with a useful networking platform

This program is open to all students without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, national or ethnic origin, physical or mental disability, marital or veteran status or any other characteristic protected by state or federal laws.
summary:
(Graduate Students Only)
As a diverse group, we aspire to promote and maintain a positive network among our students, alumni, faculty and administrators.

name: Zarb Graduate Business Association
description: The Zarb Graduate Business Association (ZGBA) is a student organization that is open to any full-time graduate student at Zarb. ZGBA is dedicated to enhancing the knowledge and skills of business students, equipping them for success in the corporate world and helping them build fulfilling careers. Members are provided with many networking opportunities with alumni, faculty and established professionals in all fields of business. Members also have the opportunity to join specialized teams within ZGBA, such as the finance team or the business analytics team.
summary: Zarb Graduate Business Association(formerly the MBA / MS Association( The primary objective of the association is to encourage students to build a network with Zarb Students as well as fellow alumni, faculty, administrators and professionals.

name: Hofstra History Club
description: Hofstra History Club meets every Monday at 8 PM in Room 302 on the Third Floor of Shapiro Family Hall. We discuss the weirdest and most niche areas of history imaginable. All are welcome!
summary: History Club meets every Monday at 8 PM on the third floor of Shapiro Family Hall/NAB, Room 302

name: National Society of Black Engineers
description: The National Society of Black Engineers (NSBE), with more than 33,000 members, is one of the largest student-governed organizations in the country. Founded in 1975, NSBE now comprises more than 450 College, Pre-College, and Technical Professional/Alumni chapters in the U.S. and abroad. NSBE aims to equip members with the contacts and skills necessary to reach their maximum professional potential. The Hofstra Chapter of the society hosts various charity and fundraising events.
summary: As a pre-professional organization, NSBE aims to provides its members with the leadership, teamwork, communication, and professional skills necessary to succeed in society.

name: South Asian Students Association
description:
The South Asian Students Association (SASA) is a cultural group that celebrates the heritage and traditions of the South Asian nations and countries.  Open to all Hofstra students, SASA hosts a series of events throughout the academic year.  

*SASA membership is for undergraduate students only 

*Please contact an Executive Board member to suggest/request an event

HEGSA strives to enhance and supplement the education of all students interested in higher ed by sponsoring social, networking, and professional development events and workshops, hosting events to educate and enlighten the campus and community about the value and resources of higher education, and creating and facilitating discussion about current trends and issues in the field of higher education on the campus, local, regional, and national levels.
summary: (Graduate Students Only) HEGSA is an organization of graduate students currently pursuing careers in the field of Higher Education.

description: A chapter of the national collegiate organization, NAfME. The mission of the National Association for Music Education is to advance music education by promoting the understanding and making of music by all. Music allows us to celebrate and preserve our cultural heritages, and also to explore the realms of expression, imagination, and creation resulting in new knowledge. Therefore, every individual should be guaranteed the opportunity to learn music and to share in musical experiences.

name: Phi Eta Sigma
description:
"Knowledge is power."

The Hofstra University chapter dedicates its time toward philanthropic work. Members organize and hold various events and programs throughout the academic year that raise funds for charities of their choice.

Chapter founded: September 8, 1996.

Meetings for the Fall 2011 semester will begin Wednesday, October 5 during common hour in CV Starr 109, and will be held bi-weekly throughout the rest of the semester.

name: Association of Students in Counseling Professions
description:
The Association of Students in Counseling Professions (a merging of the former Rehabilitation Counseling Student Association with the Counseling Club) is for graduate students in the Counseling and Mental Health Professions at Hofstra, as well as related programs. The purpose of this organization is professional development, service to the community, and social/professional networking. This purpose is achieved through professional meetings, conference attendance, community event participation, and mentoring/networking events.

Membership is open to any matriculated graduate student in the Rehabilitation Counseling, Rehabilitation Counseling in Mental Health, School Counseling, or Mental Health Counseling programs. Other graduate students in counseling and human services related fields (e.g., MFT, CATC) will be allowed membership, as well. Undergraduate students who are interested in the field of counseling and human services can attend meetings but are not considered members.

With over 100 registered members, we have established a long tradition to offer excellent performances, social events, and career services to all Hofstra Chinese students. Our mission is to unite the Chinese students, serve the local Chinese community on behalf of Hofstra and promote the communication and dialogue between China and the US.
summary: (Graduate Students Only) Our association is aiming to help Chinese students to know Hofstra, to understand American culture, and to get involved in American social life.

description: (Graduate Students Only) The Hofstra Marriage and Family Therapy Club unites students of the Master of Arts in Marriage and Family Therapy program in meetings, social and philanthropic events, and supports them in their attendance to conferences and professional development events.
summary: (Graduate Students Only) The Hofstra Marriage and Family Therapy Club unites students of the Master of Arts in Marriage and Family Therapy program in meetings, social and philanthropic events.

name: Ultimate Frisbee Club
description: Ultimate is a sport that perfectly balances athletic commitment and fun. The team is committed to teaching all who are interested in the basics of Ultimate and providing an engaging regiment of drills and workouts. We attend competitive tournaments and teach all players to use their individual skills in a competitive context. Through training, traveling, and competing together, the team builds in skill and connection. Joining the Open Ultimate Club is a truly great way to ground your Hofstra experience. If you have any interest in joining, feel free to stop by one of our practices at the intramural fields (days/times listed below) or email hofstramensultimate@gmail.com
