name: Hofstra Business Consulting Group
description:
Since its origins in 1988, the Hofstra Business Consulting Group has grown to become a powerful source of strategic consulting for growing businesses in the New York metropolitan area. HBCG consultants are M.B.A. and J.D. / M.B.A. candidates with related work experience.

The HBCG is an entity operating through Hofstra University's Frank G. Zarb School of Business. It provides a wide range of business consulting services in accounting, business computer information systems, finance, marketing, management and international business.

Faculty members representing the various disciplines within the Frank G. Zarb School of Business are often called upon for advice related to the execution of individual consulting projects.

The HBCG has built a reputation within the Long Island business community for providing quality business consulting services to its clients. The HBCG provides an excellent opportunity for growing enterprises located within the New York metropolitan area to benefit from cost-effective, efficient, quality business consulting services.

The members of the HBCG continue to work hard to maintain the reputation earned. Consultants believe the HBCG is a distinguishing feature of the Hofstra University M.B.A. Program and it is considered a factor when applying for admission.
summary: Since its origins in 1988, the Hofstra Business Consulting Group has grown to become a powerful source of strategic consulting for growing businesses in the New York metropolitan area. HBCG consultants are M.B.A. and J.D. / M.B.A. candidates

name: Phi Delta Epsilon International Medical Fraternity
description:
Phi Delta Epsilon is a co-ed International Medical Fraternity composed of a network of over 35,000 doctors, alumni, medical, and pre-medical students. Our pre-medical chapter at Hofstra University, NY Iota, has a diverse membership of undergraduate students pursuing careers in medicine. PhiDE is committed to supporting its members throughout the process and providing them with the resources and information necessary to succeed. The fraternity is founded on the principles of service, education, and most importantly, brotherhood. For more information, please visit our international website: phide.org.

Chapter Motto: Turning ambitions into physicians

name: Student Psy D Association at Hofstra
description: SPAH is the student association for the School-Community Psychology (PsyD) program at Hofstra University. Our goals are to further the educational experience of all students within the program by hosting educational, social, and community service events. The primary goal of all of our events is to help each student further develop interpersonal, leadership, and communication skills, all of which are essential to our future careers as psychologists. Research based articles are developed by the Eboard for the departmental news letter. In addition we provide at least two charitable events for the community each year. Finally, we help to bridge the gap between the professors and students, and provide all with a much needed venue for healthy downtime and breaks from our rigorous academics.
summary: (Graduate Students Only) SPAH is the student association for the School-Community Psychology (Psy.D.) program at Hofstra University.  Our goals are to further the educational experience of all students within the program.

name: Theta Tau
description:
National History

Founding

In the United States, Theta Tau is the oldest and largest Co-ed professional engineering fraternity. On October 15, 1904, the "Society of Hammer and Tongs" was founded by Erich J. Schrader, Elwin L. Vinal, William M. Lewis, and Isaac B. Hanks at University of Minnesota. The first national convention was held at the University of Minnesota, and, at the first national convention, the name was changed to Theta Tau. That convention also decided that Theta Tau would include all branches of engineering.

Today

Theta Tau currently has 92 active chapters nationwide as well as numerous colonies working hard to become chapters. Chapters meet in their respective regions for regional conferences and chapters meet nationally every other year for the national convention. Theta Tau is proud to have a strong network of over 30,000 brothers today.

Omega Beta Chapter

Theta Tau is Hofstra’s only Professional Co-ed Engineering and Computer Science Fraternity that promotes the professional growth, networking skills, critical thinking, leadership and brotherhood. The brothers of Theta Tau are chosen select few who represent an elite portion of engineers and are a part of a national community of hardworking, passionate and intelligent engineers.

The Omega Beta chapter welcomes up and coming engineers who feel the urge to be a part of something bigger than themselves. If interested in finding out more be on the lookout for upcoming meet and greets or chapter held events. Feel free to send us an email at thetatauomb@gmail.com with any questions.
summary: Theta Tau is the oldest and largest co-ed professional engineering fraternity. Theta Tau promotes professional growth, networking skills, critical thinking, leadership and brotherhood.

summary: Hofstra's PRSSA is an organization designed to help students enhance their knowledge of public relations and provide access to professional development opportunities through networking events, workshops, internships, guest speakers, and conferences.

name: The Hofstra Chronicle
description:
The Hofstra Chronicle is Hofstra University's official and independent student-run newspaper and publishes a weekly newspaper during the school year and online content year-round. The paper houses sections that include news, sports, arts and entertainment, editorials and features, as well as social media and multimedia platforms. Any views published reflect those of sources/writers and are not the opinion of the newspaper, unless otherwise noted.

If you or somebody you know is interested in joining The Chronicle, please visit our website and scroll down to fill out our involvement form. If you have any questions, send us an email at hofstrachronicle@gmail.com.

Our meetings are typically during Common Hour and we plan for our volunteering events such as National Chemistry Week at the New York Hall of Science and demonstrations for the Girl Scouts of Nassau County. description:

summary: We are a dance education club and chapter of the National Dance Education Organization (NDEO). Our goal is to work with our peers and advisors to become great dancers and dance educators.

name: The Hofstra University Dutchmen
description:
Founded in 2011, The Dutchmen are Hofstra's only all-male singing group. We pride ourselves on providing fun/engaging performances on our campus, as well as traveling long distances to collaborate with other groups! 

As a performance organization, we make it our duty to give every crowd an exciting experience. However, our main purpose is to act as a safe haven and community for guys to come together and sing! We work hard to perfect our craft, and have a great time doing it! From costumes to choreography, from long drives to short walks, from Queen to The Weeknd, there's never a dull moment in the Hofstra Dutchmen. 

To learn more, or to come out and audition, look us up on Facebook and reach out to us! We're also active on Youtube, Instagram, Twitter, and Snapchat @HofstraDutchmen. We hope to see you at an audition or performance sometime in the future!
summary: Founded in 2011, The Dutchmen are Hofstra's only all-male singing group. We work hard to provide fun/engaging performances, and have a great time doing it! To learn more about what we do, look us up on all social media platforms @HofstraDutchmen.

summary: ACDA is a group of future Music Educators coming together to explore the world of choral directing through professional development through speakers, conferences, and outreach programs.

name: WRHU, Radio Hofstra University
description:
WRHU: Participate | Hofstra University

At WRHU over three hundred current Hofstra students learn the importance of broadcasting live on an over-the-air FM radio station. Students develop broadcasting & social media skills in multimedia studios with industry standard technology (such as RCS, Wheatstone, Comrex, and Telos).

Recently, WRHU student created on-air & social media content earned 25 awards from the Press Club of Long Island, 7 awards from the New York State Broadcasters Association, 2 GRACIE awards from the Alliance for Women in Media, and a Pinnacle award from the College Media Association for “Best College Radio Station”

Over the last year:

Students reported breaking news live on WRHU FM. They conducted thousands of interviews with local, state, national, and international newsmakers and community members. Students went on site and reported from the NFL Superbowl, the Major League Baseball Hall of Fame induction in Cooperstown, the Belmont Stakes, the New Hampshire Primary, the DNC, the media filing center for the ABC TV United States Presidential Debate in Philadelphia, and local campaign headquarters on election night.

WRHU students converged audio/radio play-by-play with video crafting multiplatform (mobile/web/broadcast) live game content for Hofstra D1 teams and Brooklyn Nets NBA G-League Team the LI Nets.

WRHU Students are world famous for producing / distributing all 2024-2025 NY Islanders live NHL games audio broadcasts to WRCN FM, WRHU FM, Sirius/XM, NHL.com, and the all-new UBS Arena / NY Islanders app.

Students also produced and performed on-air roles for live Long Island Ducks baseball broadcasts.

Of course, at WRHU student air personalities entertained audiences with music shows interviewing local artists and reporting from music festivals.

For 2025-2026 WRHU student staff will create even more compelling over the air (FM) and social media content as they serve audiences quality products with mentorship from In-house professionals

WRHU is proud to partner with the SBE (Society of Broadcast Engineers) to develop top-quality tech talent (aspiring Chief Engineers) for radio stations, clusters, and networks. Many professional radio engineers visit WRHU to inspire and teach students at our “Tech Thursday” mentoring sessions.

Visit the WRHU main office and you will find the WABC TV (channel 7 New York City) Eyewitness News Long Island Bureau – one of many productive, inspirational educational partnerships and affiliations that students broadcasters at WRHU experience.

Mission Accomplished!

For more than 60 years, many Hofstra students who participated at WRHU, WVHC, and/or WHCH have gone on to professional media careers. Here is a partial list of alumni accomplishments.

WRHU has earned four Marconi Awards from the National Association of Broadcasters (including the first ever Marconi awarded to a non-commercial station). In 2024, College Media Association (CMA) named WRHU "Radio Station of the Year" for a third time. The Alliance for Women in Media, Society of Professional Journalists, Princeton Review, AP, NY State Broadcasters Association and other prestigious organizations have recognized the outstanding work of WRHU staff past and present. Learn more.
summary:
Join hundreds of students at WRHU! 

Broadcasts sports (NY Islanders / Hofstra Pride / LI Nets / LI Ducks), anchor news, host a talk show, DJ music, create social media, learn tech, become a student manager at our 4x NAB Marconi Award winning station.

name: Chi Sigma Iota
description: Chi Sigma Iota is an international academic and professional counseling honor society, dedicated to scholarship, academic excellence, and community service. Membership in the organization is only available to matriculated graduate students in the Rehabilitation Counseling, Rehabilitation Counseling in Mental Health, and Mental Health Counseling majors. Members are encouraged to attend meetings and activities throughout the semester, which may include speakers professional practice issues, counselor identity, evidence-based practices, and self-care strategies. Members also participate in community engagement activities to enhance their professional skills. Students in these majors can register as a member of Chi Sigma Iota at any time in their program; however, a minimum of 12 completed graduate level course credits with a GPA of 3.5 or above is required for induction into the honor society. Information on upcoming events will be distributed via e-mails, event flyers, and social media throughout the academic year.
summary: (Graduate Students Only) Chi Sigma Iota is an international academic and professional counseling honor society dedicated to scholarship, academic excellence, and community service.

name: Crime Scene Investigation: Hofstra
description: This club is for any Hofstra student who is interested in forensic science, crime scene investigation, and related fields. The club's primary focus is educating individuals on forensic science through miniature lectures and hands-on lab work, as well as professional networking through mentor contacts and guest speakers. We also engage in social activities and fundraisers such bake sales and Relay for Life.
summary: A club for any Hofstra student who is interested in forensic science and crime scene investigation!

summary:
(Graduate Students Only) 

IOPSA is an organization for I/O Psychology Students. Our goal is to foster a collaborative environment, provide networking and career building opportunities, and much more.

name: Graduate Students in Clinical Psychology
description:
The GSCP represents Graduate Students in Clinical Psychology at Hofstra (specifically within the Ph.D. program). Our board is comprised of second year students who are elected during the first year. GSCP positions include: President, Vice President, Treasurer, Secretary and 2 Class Representatives. GSCP works closely with our Advisor and Director of Clinical Training Dr. Sanderson, and with past and future Presidents to maintain continuity.

We are dedicated to building professional connections within the program, as well as with alumni and other professionals in the field. Additionally, we aim to enhance our clinical training through a variety of educational events in which we can learn from the expertise and experiences of others. We believe this approach unifies and strengthens our program. The events that GSCP plans or facilitates typically include: 1st Year Orientation, Professional Networking at ABCT, Interview Day for Prospective Students, End of Semester Celebrations, Alumni Panels, Clinical Colloquia and Case Conferences.

2021/22 GSCP leadership:
President: Jennifer Babekov 
Vice President: Arielle Klopsis
Treasurer: Janice Lu
Secretary: Arielle Snow
Members at large: Nahal Talasazan & Mitch Mazzone

name: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated
description:
Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated is an international service organization that was founded at Howard University in Washington, D.C. in 1908. It is the oldest Greek-lettered organization established by African-American college-educated women.

Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated is comprised of a nucleus of 260,000 members in graduate and undergraduate chapters in the United States, the U.S. Virgin Islands, the Caribbean, Canada, Japan, Germany, Korea, and on the continent of Africa.

Its membership is comprised of distinguished women who boast excellent academic records, proven leadership skills and are involved in the global community through advocacy and service. Alpha Kappa Alpha has dedicated itself to improving the quality of life for citizens worldwide and promoting peace.
summary: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated is an international service organization that was founded at Howard University in Washington, D.C. in 1908. It is the oldest Greek-lettered organization established by African-American college-educated women.

  • Inclusiveness
  • Teamwork
summary: ALPFA is a national organization that helps students network with professionals, build business skills necessary to succeed, and ultimately aids students in obtaining internships as well as jobs. ALPFA has over 95,000 members nationwide.

name: Beta Beta Beta Biological Honor Society
description:
“Beta Beta Beta Biological Honor Society is a society for students, particularly for undergraduates. It seeks to encourage scholarly attainment in this field of learning by reserving its regular membership for those who achieve superior academic records and who indicate special aptitude for and major interest in the life sciences. It desires to cultivate intellectual interest in the natural sciences and to promote a better appreciation of the value of biological study and thus welcomes into associate membership all those students who are interested in biology. Beta Beta Beta also endeavors to extend the boundaries of man’s knowledge of nature by encouraging new discoveries through scientific investigation and to this end encourages undergraduate students to begin research work and report their findings in the journal of the society, BIOS. It emphasizes, therefore, a three-fold program: stimulation of scholarship, dissemination of scientific knowledge, and promotion of biological research.” - Beta Beta Beta Biological Honor Society Handbook, 2019

The Alpha Lambda Chapter of Beta Beta Beta (TriBeta) is the Biological Honors Society at Hofstra University. The group strives to have at least one event each semester to raise money for important biology-related causes as well as to foster community in Hofstra’s Biology Department. Recent events have included hosting the TriBeta NE-1 Regional Conference in 2019, the Haunted House through Gittleson as a fundraiser for Cohen's Children's Hospital, and the Biology Mentoring Program. TriBeta members are encouraged to apply for the National Research Grants and to present their research at the annual regional conferences. You can follow their activities on the Hofstra TriBeta Instagram (see link below).

New members are inducted in the Spring semester each academic year. Induction is by invitation only and requires a 3.25 GPA in all biology courses, a 3.0 overall GPA, and at least 3 Biology courses completed at Hofstra, one of which must be above the introductory level.
summary: TriBeta is an honor society for students majoring in the biological sciences. These students are dedicated to improving the understanding and appreciation of biological study and extending boundaries of human knowledge through scientific research.

name: Creative Arts Therapy Club at Hofstra
description: CATCH is a club for Graduate Students of the Creative Arts Therapy program at Hofstra. Our meetings are intended for the sole purpose of recreation, support, collaboration, and socialization.
summary: CATCH is a club for Graduate Students of the Creative Arts Therapy program at Hofstra.

name: Order of Omega
description:
To RECOGNIZE those fraternity men and women who have attained a high standard of leadership in interfraternity activities, to ENCOURAGE them to continue along this line, and to INSPIRE others to strive for similar conspicuous attainment;

To UNITE outstanding fraternity men and women to create an organization which will help to mold the sentiment of the institution on questions of local and intercollegiate fraternity affairs;

To BRING TOGETHER members of the faculty, alumni, and student members of the institution's fraternities and sororities on a basis of mutual interest, understanding and helpfulness;

To help CREATE an atmosphere where ideas and issues can be discussed openly across Greek lines and to help work out solutions.
summary: Order of Omega is a leadership honor society for members of Fraternity & Sorority organizations who have exemplified high standards in the areas of scholarship, leadership, involvement within their respective orga

name: Educational Leadership Doctoral Fellows
description: The Educational Leadership Doctoral Fellows club has two purposes: 1) to develop and sustain a sense of community among doctoral students in the program and 2) to support their development as researchers and leaders in ways that supplement their traditional coursework.   Virtually all the doctoral students in this program work full-time, many in educational leadership positions, while attending Hofstra part-time, and managing family responsibilities. The organization aims to provide stimulating intellectual and social activities that will bring students together outside of the classroom for the kind of collegial conversations with their peers and faculty that foster networking and provide the kind of personal, academic, and professional support that sustain students through a demanding degree program.  Additionally, the club will support activities that introduce members to scholarly organizations in the field of educational leadership and encourage them to participate as practitioner researchers.
summary: (Graduate Students Only) The Educational Leadership Doctoral Fellows is a student organization for doctoral students in all phases of the Ed.D. program in Educational and Policy Leadership.

name: Future Healthcare Leaders of Hofstra University
description: The Future Healthcare Leaders of Hofstra University is an organization that works to ensure opportunities within our organization and local community for knowledge sharing and career development among future healthcare management professionals. FHL achieves this by providing workshops, lectures, trips and networking opportunities to Hofstra students. Additionally, we hope to continually improve student readiness for careers in healthcare by providing forums for the discussion of the healthcare industry through knowledge, communication, and leadership.
summary: (Graduate Students Only) The Future Healthcare Leaders of Hofstra University works to ensure opportunities for knowledge sharing and career development among future healthcare management professionals within our organization and local community.

name: Society of Physics Students
description:
This club has been set up to provide networking and educational opportunities to all students, especially those majoring in and/or minoring in Physics. We are also affiliated with the global Society of Physics Students (SPS). Physics majors in this club also have a chance to become a part of the Sigma Pi Sigma Physics Honors Society.


We frequently have meetings discussing opportunities open to students pursuing the study of physics, including research and internships, as well as networking opportunities with alumni and faculty. There are trips to local private and national laboratories, and presentations of undergraduate research.


There is occasional recreational coordination with other science pre-professional student organizations.
summary: Hofstra's chapter of the Society of Physics Students, is a group of multicultural Physics-lovers (of many different majors) working together to advocate for Physics education and appreciation among the Hofstra community.

name: Special Education Graduate Association
description: SEGA is an Association that provides Hofstra Special Education graduate students an opportunity to come together and learn more about special education. SEGA provides opportunities for the members to participate in activities related to the field of special education as well as attend various learning opportunities.  SEGA is also an opportunity to develop friendships and build professional networks. SEGA hosts and co-sponsors a variety of activities throughout the year and encourages an active and engaged membership.
summary: (Graduate Students Only) A graduate student organization that provides opportunities for members to participate in activities related to the field of special education, as well as attend various learning opportunities.

name: National Honor Society for Dance Arts
description: National Honor Society for Dance Arts - Students who have a minimum GPA of 3.5 and are BFA or BA Dance, BS Dance Ed, Minor in Dance or have taken 18 credits or more of dance can apply to be in this honor society
summary: National Honor Society for Dance Arts - Students who have a minimum GPA of 3.5 and are BFA or BA Dance, BS Dance Ed, Minor in Dance or have taken 18 credits or more of dance can apply to be in the honor society.

name: Hofstra Chess Club
description: Hofstra Chess Club shall promote the game of chess within the Hofstra University community. HCC will facilitate and teach the rules, tactics and strategies of the chess game to the Hofstra University community. This organization will seek to bring enjoyment to every member through participation in the royal game. This organization supports any activities promoting chess in any community. Such activities may include, but is not limited to tournaments, team matches and exhibitions.
summary: At Hofstra Chess Club you will be able to learn how to play chess, discover new strategies and tactics of the game, giving you the opportunity to win. HCC will participate in various tournaments giving every member opportunity to develop their skills.

name: Society of Public Health Advocates
description: The Society of Public Health Advocates (SOPHA) is a Public/Community Health graduate student run organization at Hofstra University. SOPHA looks to encourage environmental awareness, disease prevention, and health promotion through advocacy both at Hofstra and in the surrounding communities. The purpose of the organization is to collaborate among the MSCH and MPH programs to establish opportunities of involvement in the promotion of public health on and off campus. SOPHA strives to bring awareness to the social determinants of health to minimize health disparities through community outreach and engagement.
summary: (Graduate Students Only) SOPHA is a Public/Community Health graduate student run organization at Hofstra University. SOPHA looks to encourage heath prevention, promotion, and awareness.

name: Rock Climbing Club
description:
Welcome! We are Hofstra Rock Climbing. Climbs are held about twice a month around New York City. We cycle between Brooklyn Boulders Queensbridge, Movement at Long Island City and Gowanus, and The Gravity Vault at Westbury and Melville.

Feel free to join our GroupMe and add us on Instagram. This is how we distribute the majority of our information and post sign-ups for climbs.

                                                                              GroupMe

                                                                             Instagram
summary: Hofstra Rock Climbing hosts climbs twice a month around New York City and on Long Island.

name: American String Teachers Association
description: As a collegiate chapter of the American String Teachers Association (ASTA), we focus on preparing the music education majors of Hofstra University to become string teachers.  We host professional speakers to discuss their string experiences, as well as hosting student-run meetings discussing our challenges and ideas, as well as opportunities to play our primary and secondary instruments.  Student members will have networking opportunities, volunteer experience, and the ability to attend conferences and workshops. Through this experience, student members will gain knowledge about how to be a string teacher.
summary: As a collegiate chapter of the American String Teachers Association (ASTA), we focus on string teacher education through professional speakers and workshops, providing networking opportunities, and offering volunteer work.

name: Physician Assistant Club
description: To educate and inform students about the Physician Assistant Career and to provide recourses to students on campus interested in pursuing this path. Club is open to all Hofstra Students interested in the Physician Assistant Career.
summary: To educate and inform students about the Physician Assistant Career and to provide volunteer services to the local community and students on campus. Club is open to all Hofstra Students interested in the Physician Assistant Career.

name: UNICEF @ Hofstra
description: "UNICEF@Hofstra" is a nationally-recognized UNICEF Campus Initiative Club. This Club advocates and informs the Hofstra community about UNICEF's initiatives for child survival across the world. We participate in popular UNICEF campaigns, including Trick-or-Treat for UNICEF and the UNICEF Tap Project. We also host our own fundraising events. All money raised is donated to UNICEF, exclusively.
summary: "UNICEF @ Hofstra" is a nationally-recognized UNICEF Campus Initiative Club. Our fundraising club advocates and informs the Hofstra community about UNICEF humanitarian initiatives. For UNICEF, the U.S. improves children's lives in over 150 countries.

name: RHYTHMiX
description: This is a tap dance club, open to tap dancers of all levels. Each week, we focus on strengthening technique, learning combinations, and improving improvisation skills. We also work on choreography for a few performances each semester. Also, we bring in several professional tap dancers each year to teach free master classes.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to educate the Hofstra Community on the history and technique of tap dancing.

name: Circle K International
description:
Hofstra Circle K International is the collegiate level of the larger Kiwanis International organization. We strive to promote our core values of leadership, fellowship and service in all our members.

If you were a member of Aktion Club or Key Club, we encourage you to continue your involvement with the organization and continue serving others!
summary: To provide constructive opportunities for students to become involved on their campus and communities through service work to others in need.

name: Her Campus Hofstra
description: Do you want to write for one of the most prominent online college magazines? Her Campus gives you the opportunity to write about what interests you while strengthening your skills, build a writing presence for yourself online and meet new friends with similar passions. We write about news, pop culture, beauty, style, life and more! HC Hofstra is a great organization to put on your resume if you're looking to break into the world of journalism or public relations or just write for fun. Past and current members of this chapter have gone on to intern for Cosmo, Marie Claire, Inked, The Knot, Instyle, US Weekly, Shape, CNN, Bust and many more. Check us out: Hercampus.com/Hofstra
summary: HC Hofstra is one of the most prominent online college magazines. Our publication covers news, style, beauty, pop culture, health, music, life, career and much more. We provide college students with the opportunity to build a strong online portfolio.

name: Hofstra English Society
description: Hofstra English Society (HES) is a student-run organization dedicated to all things literary! We produce Font, Hofstra's student run literary magazine that publishes student-written work, art, and photography. We also host a variety of literary themed games and events. If you have an interest in joining, please email at hofenglishsociety@gmail.com or follow us at hofenglishsociety on Instagram!
summary: The Hofstra English Society is a student club that holds events relevant to the topics of literature, writing, and publishing.

name: Zeta Phi Eta
description: The Hofstra Chapter of Zeta Phi Eta, a national professional communications fraternity is an organization based on developing meaningful and lasting relationships within the Lawrence Herbert School of Communication. Through various career oriented workshops, seminars and guest lectures the group focuses on providing driven communications majors the opportunity to build lasting relationships with like-minded individuals.
summary: The Hofstra Chapter of Zeta Phi Eta, a national pre-professional fraternity works to develop meaningful and lasting professional relationships and connections within the Lawrence Herbert School of Communication.

name: Hofstra University Chapter of the Student Academy of the American Academy of Physician Assistants
description:
(Graduate Students Only) The Hofstra University Chapter of SAAAPA serves as the official organization for the students of the Hofstra University Physician Assistant Studies Program.  

The mission of the organization is to encourage academic achievement and clinical excellence, to educate the public and promote the Physician Assistant (PA) as a member of the health care delivery team, and to demonstrate Hofstra University’s commitment to training future PA Providers. 

The Hofstra University Chapter of SAAAPA operates in conjunction with the New York State Society of Physician Assistants (NYSSPA) and the American Academy of Physician Assistants (AAPA).  The organization values community involvement and participates in campus blood drives and hosts multiple fundraisers and food drives each year.
summary: (Graduate Students Only) Hofstra SAAAPA is the official organization for the students of the Hofstra University Physician Assistant Studies Program.  Membership is open to graduate students who are currently enrolled in the...

name: Hofstra University Pre-Dental Association
description: Hofstra University's Pre-Dental Student Association aims to provide guidance and internship/volunteer opportunities for students in STEM and the pre-dental field. We hope to inform our community through events about the importance of oral health and how it can affect our bodies long term. We want to help Hofstra's pre-dental students feel supported and help them every step of the way!
summary: This club seeks to provide its members with the opportunity not only to gain a wide variety of information in the fields of dentistry and oral health, but to assist in the finding of volunteer or internship positions at professional practices.

name: American Society of Civil Engineers Hofstra Chapter
description: Founded in 1852, the American Society of Civil Engineers (ASCE) represents more than 145,000 members of the civil engineering profession worldwide and is America’s oldest national engineering society.  As the Hofstra University  Student Chapter we participate in everything the Long Island Branch of ASCE has to offer, ranging from technical lectures to site tours. The chapter encourages all engineering students be involved in ASCE during their time at Hofstra and after. The involvement opportunities while at Hofstra include technical lectures, steel bridge and concrete canoe competitions, resume workshops, site tours, guest speakers and other events. Partaking in Hofstra University Student Chapter gives the opportunity to be a part of ASCE younger members forum (YMF) after graduation.
summary: An academic organization that focuses on topics and events related to the field of civil engineering.  The club attends technical lectures that focus on various innovative projects in the field of civil engineering.

name: Hofstra Esports
description:
Hofstra Esports is a club all about the discussion and enjoyment of video games! Whether you wish to play on one of our several competitive teams, or simply enjoy games casually, our club is a community for players of all skill levels!

 

If you are more on the casual side, we have plenty of opportunities for you to meet and play with others! We host a Super Smash Bros. tournament every week called Smash with Pride that is open to all students and skill levels. We also host several different casual events throughout the year, including a Minecraft server, a Speedrunning showcase, and many more! Our Discord server also provides a place where our club and community can discuss all things video games. 

 

For our more competitive gamers, we have several active teams that you can choose from. Currently, our lineup of competitive teams includes Rocket League, Smash Bros., Valorant, and Splatoon. We've also previously had teams for Counter Strike, Overwatch, and League of Legends. See a game you're interested in? Don't be afraid to get involved! Don't see the game you like? You can start your own team! All of our teams are created and run by students!

 

Whether you are casual or competitive, Hofstra Esports is the home for all things video games at Hofstra!

 

It is important that ALL new members sign on to our club Discord, as its there where we make club announcements and is the main place we interact with members. Click HERE for the link to our Discord, so you can get involved!
summary: Hofstra Esports is a club all about the discussion and enjoyment of video games! Whether you wish to play on one of our several competitive teams, or simply enjoy games casually, our club is a community for players of all skill levels!

name: Hofstra Global Medical Brigades
summary: (Graduate students only) Global Medical Brigades, GMB, is a secular, non-profit, student run volunteer organization who aims to provide sustained health care to under-served communities all over the world.

name: Hofstra Ballroom and Latin Dance
description: Hofstra Ballroom and Latin Dance promotes the activity of ballroom dancing in both a social and competitive environment. We teach various styles at all levels, travel and compete with other universities on the East Coast, and put on performances around campus. Any student may participate regardless of major or prior experience. Our organization includes both a Social and Competitive team and is open to all, regardless of prior dance experience!
summary: Hofstra Ballroom and Latin Dance promotes ballroom dancing in both a social and competitive environment. We teach various styles at all levels, travel & compete with other universities on the East Coast, and put on performances around campus.

name: Hofstra Golf Club
description: Golf is one of the hardest games in the world. We realize this and want to have fun through the challenging moments. Within the Hofstra Golf Club, students will be able to have a place to enjoy the sport for what it is. We look to host different golf related activities such as practicing at a driving range, playing minigolf, and even getting out on the course for a nice round of golf! Get in touch with us via email golf.club@pride.hofstra.edu or reach out to any of our executive board members!
summary: The Hofstra Golf Club aspires to foster an environment where students can expand their golfing desires with peers regardless of skill level.

name: 180 Degrees Consulting
description:
** Due to consulting cycles with clients, our application for new members closes within the first month of the beginning of a given semester. If you join our GetInvolved, you will be emailed when the next cycle opens up. Fall 2022 has already begun and new members cannot be added ** 

 

180 Degrees Consulting gives college students the opportunity to work on real life consulting projects. We typically work on 2-3 projects per semester and each student is assigned to work on one of those projects. Although consulting is a business process, all majors are encouraged to become student consultants, as regardless of area of study, 180 Degrees brings strong-minded analytical thinkers together to help solve various issues faced by non-profit organizations around the Hofstra community.
summary: 180 Degrees Consulting at Hofstra is a branch of the world's largest student consultancy. With more than 50 branches all over the world, 180 Degrees works with socially conscious organizations to help them achieve a greater social impact.

name: Student Computing Services
summary: Student Computing Services assists students with software questions and training.

name: Motorsports Engineering Club
description:
Hofstra Motorsport Engineering Club (MEC):

Come Join Hofstra's one and only Car Club. We are a society of automotive enthusiast and engineers. Whether you: are a car enthusiast, or just interested in cars ALL are welcome to join. All builds, Cars/trucks  are welcome, you do not need a car to join just a passion for the automotive world. 

We Host/Do everything from:

--Car shows (one per semester on campus)

--Mini Car Meets on Campus

--Booths at Car Shows/Events

--Car Cruises (Bear Mountain state park, Montauk State Park, etc.)

--Gran Turismo Tournaments, featuring our racing simulator rigs (Wheel, Pedal Box, Shifter, Bucket Seat)

--Donation Campaigns (Charity Car washes and Toys-For-Tots)

--And much more

Meetings are every Wednesday during common hour in Adams Hall RM 109 1:20-2:20pm, though be on the lookout because we often meet at Hofstra USA parking lot for our Park and Chills

Follow us on Instagram, and please inquire to join our club GroupMe!

Instagram @HofstraMEC
summary:
Hofstra MEC (Motorsport Engineering Club) Is Hofstra's one and only car club on campus, we do everything from: Car Shows, Car Cruises, Charity Events, Racing Simulator Tournaments and so much more.

DM us @hofstramec for more information!

name: Alpha Phi Omega
description:
Alpha Phi Omega is the single most represented co-educational intercollegiate service organization in the United States. Since 1925, more than 525,000 members on over 375 campuses, continue to provide more service on more campuses than any other collegiate service organization. It is the vision of Alpha Phi Omega to create inclusive communities for a more peaceful world in which to make a life, by developing leaders, uniting members through friendship, and rendering service to all.

 

The Alpha Eta Tau chapter was founded at Hofstra on May 1st, 2016, and currently resides under the Professional Fraternity Council. We are an interest-based organization, which means you can join both APO and another organization -- pre-professional, social, or both. As a chapter, we earn over 800 hours of community service each semester. We host events nearly every day that represent what our organization stands for: service, fellowship, leadership, and DEI (diversity, equity, inclusion).

Students seeking membership must attend Recruitment Week, which takes place every September and February.

 

For more information on our organization and Recruitment Week, check out our Instagram @apohofstra or contact us at alphaphiomega.hofstra@gmail.com with questions.
summary: Alpha Phi Omega (ΑΦΩ or APO) is a gender-inclusive, international community service organization. Our founding principles are Leadership, Friendship and Service.

name: Hofstra Amnesty International
description: At Amnesty International, we firmly believe in the power of the Declaration of Human Rights. Hofstra Amnesty International aims to engage people within the Hofstra community - be it students, faculty, or neighbors - in the political process of ensuring all human rights are protected worldwide. Through workshops, panel discussions, conferences, and fun activities, we aim to make human rights education and activism as accessible to our community as possible. Join us in our journey!
summary: Hofstra Amnesty International is a body of students who seek to make human rights education and activism accessible to all.

name: African Student Association
description:
We are a pan-african organization with the intention of fostering a community in which Africans, African Americans and anybody interested can learn and cultivate an understanding of the African continent and the diaspora.

This organization is open to all students without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, national or ethnic origin, physical or mental disability, marital or veteran status or any other characteristic protected by state or federal laws.
summary: The mission of ASA is to to educate the Hofstra community about Africa’s rich culture (food, fashion, dance, etc.) as well as political & social aspects of the continent. We want Hofstra's campus to embrace Africa! Anyone is welcome to join the club.

name: Table Tennis Club
description:
Creating a place for the Hofstra community - students, graduate or undergraduate, and faculty - to come and play the fun game of table tennis together. We want to make it easy for members to meet other competitive players and to provide guidance for those wanting to improve. All members are welcome to have fun and enjoy playing whether it be competitive or just for fun. 

 

Join our group me chat for regular updates on meetings and for socializing with other members:
https://groupme.com/join_group/97613761/uQcXX5Jb
summary: Place for the Hofstra community - students, graduate or undergraduate, and faculty - to come and play the fun game of table tennis together. Come hang out and meet people, whether you want to play seriously or for fun!

name: Hofstra Culinary Club
description: The Hofstra Culinary Club (HCC) is for students passionate about cooking, baking, dietary health and exploring global cuisines. Our mission is to bring together food enthusiasts of all skill levels to share recipes, learn new culinary techniques, and celebrate diverse cultures through food. Whether you're a seasoned chef or just starting in the kitchen, HCC offers hands-on workshops, food tastings, and social events that spark new experiences and connections. Join us to experiment with flavors, discover new dishes, and connect with fellow food lovers who share your passion for all things culinary!
summary: Hofstra Culinary Club (HCC) is a welcoming space for students passionate about cooking, baking, dietary health, and global cuisines. We host hands-on workshops, food tastings, and fun events where all skill levels can enjoy!

name: SheEOs
description:
The purpose of this organization is to empower students to accomplish their goals at the collegiate level and beyond. Furthermore, the organization strives to prepare students for the workplace intellectually and socially through group discussions, workshops, and networking opportunities. The organization has a special focus on women; therefore, SheEOs works hard to provide a safe community where all students can respectfully participate in discussions about gender-based issues in the workplace.

SheEOs' number one priority through their meetings and events is to encourage students to become well-rounded young professionals and strong women in business.

This program is open to all students without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, national or ethnic origin, physical or mental disability, marital or veteran status or any other characteristic protected by state or federal laws.
summary: Women in business undergraduate organization that helps students build networks, learn new business concepts, and develop themselves professionally.

name: American Society of Mechanical Engineers
description:
Hofstra's Mechanical Engineering Club focuses on hands-on projects involving mechanical, aeronautics, and electrical components. All majors are welcome!

 

This program is open to all students regardless of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, national or ethnic origin, physical or mental disability, marital or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by state or federal laws.

 

Join the GroupMe to learn more!:

https://groupme.com/join_group/106431384/mFqZ0hF0
summary: Mechanical Engineering Club with a focus on hands-on projects involving mechanical, aeronautics, and electrical components. All majors welcome.

name: Phi Kappa Theta Fraternity
description: Phi Kappa Theta - NY Alpha Omega strives to develop men to be effective leaders who passionately serve society, Fraternity, and God. As an organization, we place strong emphasis on academics, community service, and campus involvement. We have regular service events, both alone and with other organizations; and many of our members are leaders in other organizations, clubs, teams, etc. Additionally, as a historically and traditionally Catholic fraternity, we focus on developing the spiritual aspect of each brother. While brothers need not be Catholic or Christian, they must examine themselves and the world around them to assess a greater worldview and realize that our own selves may not be the greatest aspects of our lives. Overall, brothers of Phi Kappa Theta are asked to be productive and selfless members of society, following the Fraternity motto, "Give, expecting nothing thereof."
summary: Welcome to the Phi Kappa Theta NY Alpha Omega Chapter at Hofstra. We strive to actively develop men to be effective leaders who passionately serve society, Fraternity, and God.

name: The Netherlands
description:
(Delft, Groningen, Hague, Leiden, Rotterdam, Tilberg, Utrecht, Orange, Rensselaer, Breukelen, Amsterdam)
This complex is divided into two courtyards with a total of 11 two-story houses. Reserved specifically for first year students each house accommodates approximately 55 residents. All suites in this area contain two double bedrooms, a bathroom and a lounge. The Netherlands core is also home to a dining facility, a satellite office for Academic Advisement, Counseling Services, Residential Programs and academic classroom space.

(Newport, Plymouth, Williamsburg, Dover, Portsmouth, Providence, Quincy, Salem, Cambridge, Hampton, Hempstead, New York, Norwich, Jamestown)


The largest residence hall complex on the campus has three courtyards with a total of 14 two-story houses. Individual houses accommodate between 55 to 65 residents per house. Each house has suites containing either two double bedrooms, a bathroom and a lounge; two double rooms, a bathroom and no lounge or two double bedrooms, a single bedroom and a bathroom with no lounge; two single rooms, a bathroom and a lounge. New York and Providence Houses are reserved specifically for first year students. Plymouth house is reserved for our graduate/law residence students.

name: Center for Career Design & Development
description:
The Center for Career Design & Development is a team of professionals who put students first. We achieve our vision of empowering all Hofstra students toward meaningful careers and lives through one-on-one appointments for career counseling available in person, via Zoom, and telephone anytime the university is open.

Career Coaching
Career coaching covers a range of valuable topics, including:

  • Uncovering your strengths
  • Best-fit majors and career paths
  • Internship and study-abroad opportunities
  • Searching for a job
  • Applying for graduate school
  • Interview preparation
  • Polishing résumés and cover letters

Programming & Events
We also partner to provide programming for the entire campus that empowers Hofstra Students to uncover their strengths, determine their path, and tell their story. If your student-run organization would like to partner with the Center for Career Design & Development to host a workshop or program, call us or submit a program request.

Connections
Once you are ready to share your strengths and tell your story, the Center for Career Design & Development facilitates connections with employers both online via Handshake and in person at Career Fairs, On Campus Recruiting, Information Sessions, Service Networking, and other creative events.
summary: Hofstra Career Center

name: Center for Academic Excellence
description: The Center for Academic Excellence (CAE) is dedicated to help students achieve success in their academics and overall college experience. The CAE promotes improved academic performance and student persistence through strategies that lead to active learning, engagement, and self-regulation. The CAE staff work closely with students, faculty, and other departments within the Division of Student Affairs and throughout the University to proactively and efficiently identify and implement strategies to bolster students’ academic and personal success. CAE services, including the University Tutorial Program (UTP) and Academic Success Programs (ASP), are available to all enrolled undergraduate students.
summary: The Center for Academic Excellence (CAE) is dedicated to help students achieve success in their academics and overall college experience. The CAE promotes improved academic performance and student persistence.

name: Zarb Accounting Graduate Association
description:
Greeting all Accounting and Taxation Majors!

 

I hope your studies are going well and you are all healthy and well as we kick off the Spring’21 semester.

 

We look forward to being a part of ZAGA (Zarb’s Accounting Graduate Association)  with you and take advantage of all it has to offer!

 

We are currently recruiting for both general members and to fill the E-Board position of Secretary!

 

Who are we?

ZAGA aims to provide its members with networking opportunities both with fellow students in your major and with professionals from the Accounting and Financial industries. It is our goal to provide you with confidence, advice, and support as you navigate graduate school and enter into or transition through the work force. Making decisions pertaining to your career can generate lots of questions, this is your place to ask them!

 

What do we do?

We will be introducing you to many of the various careers for accounting and tax majors through guest speaker events from industry leaders and experts. Our goal is to provide our members with insight and a better understanding of:

  • How to break into alternative career paths for Accounting and Tax majors in growing industries.
  • How to differentiate yourself as an entrepreneur with an accounting or tax background by learning about the various ways to obtain the necessary experience and expertise to build your own book of business
  • How to break into the more traditional public accounting route with the big four.

 

We are aiming to hold virtual events Wednesday Evenings 1 to 2 times a month.

  • We would like our members to attend each event so we will be providing ZAGA raffle tickets for each event you attend.
  • We will be doing a drawing at the end of the semester and the prize will likely be a $500 award to be used for education expenses.

 

We are looking to get two to four quality guest speakers this semester and we will keep you updated on what industry experts we will be bringing in as well as what other events you should expect!

  • Area’s of focus for this semester will likely be:
    • Crypto Asset Taxation.
    • Small to Mid-Size Firm Exposure
      • How to stand out, differentiate yourself in a unique practice, and advice from industry veterans on how work your way to partner.
    • Alternative Tax/Accounting Careers:
      • Careers in Investment Banking, Alternative Investments, Corporate Accounting, Deal Advisory (M&A), Real Estate, etc.

 

When can you join us?

Our first event is right around the corner and it will be:

  • ZAGA Networking Night – Wednesday, February 24th
    • Introductory meeting to meet and network with the members of ZAGA. We believe it is important to build relationships with your peers throughout your graduate studies and career and encourage you to join us!
    • This will be a very casual event to start this semester off and we will be drawing a raffle where the winner will receive a fun prize!

 

How can you reach us?

If you are interested in joining ZAGA don’t hesitate to reach out to us and we will get you on the roster:

  • Email Address: hofstrazaga@gmail.com
  • GetInvolved Link https://hofstra.campuslabs.com/engage/organization/zaga
    • Contact our board and request membership through this link!

If you have any questions pertaining to the organization don’t hesitate to reach out to us through the ZAGA email address or reach out to me personally at tsoliman1@pride.hofstra.edu.

 

We look forward to networking with and learning with you all this and the following semesters during our graduate studies!

 

 

We are not collecting dues for this semester during the pandemic to help make our club accessible during these hectic times and due to the fact that our planned events will be organized over zoom this semester.
summary: The Zarb Accounting Graduate Association is a graduate association open to all graduate majors, focused on the professional growth of the accounting skills of their members.

name: American Cancer Society on Campus at Hofstra University

Through campus-wide initiatives such as Relay for Life, awareness campaigns, and volunteer opportunities, we bring the Hofstra community together to stand united in the fight against cancer. By combining education, advocacy, and service, we empower students to take on leadership roles, support those in need, and help build a compassionate community committed to creating a world without cancer.

Through collaboration with faculty, student organizations, and diverse student veterans, the SVA seeks to enhance the college experience, promote leadership, and ensure veterans’ voices are heard.
summary: The Hofstra SVA aims to support and advocate on behalf of student veterans/service members on campus, as they transition into their academic and professional careers. We are a fully inclusive organization, and welcome all Hofstra students and faculty.

name: Pi Lambda Phi Fraternity
description:
Pi Lambda Phi was founded in 1895 at Yale University, with the NY Alpha Tau Chapter being established at Hofstra in Spring 2016. Pilam is composed of the most diverse and open-minded brothers on campus; Pi Lambda Phi was founded as the very first “Non-Sectarian” Fraternity; meaning that our brotherhood does not discriminate one’s race, religion, creed, or sexual orientation. At Pilam, we work hard, and play harder. We bring out the best each in each other, using our unique identities to offer a lot to not just the community, but to you. We hold ourselves to the Five Pillars, our core values being Character, Commitment, Finance, Leadership, and Scholarship. With these in mind, our desire is to bring like-minded and fun-loving individuals who are looking to fulfill their untapped potential. Socially, we aim to bring people together, helping those who wish to be better communicators and getting the best out of their college experience. Fraternally, one will find a warm and genuine welcome; at Pilam, we treat fellow brothers as more than just a number. Our alumni networks also afford opportunities for networking and career development. Our Philanthropy is the Elimination of Prejudice, with our mission being to spread awareness and highlight the importance of eradicating racism and prejudice. Besides all of this, our brothers bond over many different hobbies and interests; we frequently attend Hofstra sporting events, hold our own games of football, basketball, and soccer with brothers, watch sports games frequently, going out to our favorite restaurants and trying new ones often to catch the playoffs, help each other study, go to the beach, and generally support one another as we make our way through college. Many brothers have diverse interests in music, sports, film, style, food, and other hobbies.

In short, Pi Lambda Phi can be best summed up by our motto: “Not Four Years, But A Lifetime”. To us, its not just a motto. We aim to have all brothers take something meaningful away from their time in a fraternity. College eventually does end and as we make our journeys, we will carry memories with us forever. At Pi Lambda Phi, we aim to make those lasting memories and establish lifelong bonds.
summary: Pi Lambda Phi was founded in 1895 at Yale University, with the NY Alpha Tau Chapter being established at Hofstra in Spring 2016. Pilam is composed of diverse and open-minded brothers. Pi Lambda Phi was founded as the very first “Non-Sectarian” Fraternity

If you wish to make new friends and expand your board gaming experience beyond the likes of Monopoly and Scrabble, HofTop is the club for you! 

Join our club's Discord!

description:
For more than six decades, Society of Women Engineers has encouraged a gender-inclusive environment in engineering disciplines. A national organization, SWE’s mission is to “empower women to achieve their full potential in careers as engineers and leaders, expand the image of the engineering and technology professions as a positive force in improving the quality of life, and demonstrate the value of diversity and inclusion”.

Our organization is centered around a passion for our members' success and continues to evolve with the challenges and opportunities reflected in today's exciting engineering and technology specialties. description:
The Student Organization Of Animal Rights is a passionate and dedicated group of individuals committed to advocating for the ethical treatment and welfare of animals. Our mission is to raise awareness about animal rights issues, promote cruelty-free lifestyles, and take action to protect and support animals in need.

Through educational discussions, activism, and community outreach, we strive to inspire change and encourage humane treatment of all living beings. Our activities include hosting guest speakers, organizing protests and petitions, volunteering and promoting sustainable and ethical choices.

Whether you are an animal lover, or simply someone who wants to make a difference, our club welcomes everyone who believes in kindness, compassion, and justice for animals. Join us in making the world a better place!
summary: Passionate about animals? So are we! Stand up for the voiceless! Join our club to be apart of a community dedicated to protecting animals, spreading awareness and advocating for their rights!

name: The Philosophy Department
description:
In the Department of Philosophy students examine fundamental issues such as the nature of truth, existence, justice, goodness, beauty, and knowledge. Through a wide range of courses – from courses on Plato to courses on “Free Will,” on “Harry Potter and Philosophy,” on “Ethics and Animals,” and many more – students become creative problem solvers who can make sense of complex information in a modern world.  These valuable skills have helped our students pursue successful careers in many fields including business, politics, law, medicine, and the arts, as well as advanced graduate study.

 

Students can also take philosophy courses as part of an interdisciplinary program such as public policy and public service; technology and public policy; urban ecology; criminology and forensic studies; sustainability; philosophy of law; scientific reasoning and data analysis; philosophy, society and business; and more.  Pre-health majors can also take philosophy as a concentration.
summary: This is the department page for the Philosophy Department.

name: First Generation Pride
description: Are you the first in your family to go to college? Around 1 in 3 college students are. We're here to create opportunities for first-gens to connect with each other, with faculty, and with staff to learn about campus resources and to engage in social and educational programming. In short, we want to help you succeed and feel at home on Hofstra's campus!
summary: First Generation students are defined as students where neither of their parents has completed a 4 year college degree in the United States.

name: Department of Economics
description:
Economics studies how people, individually and collectively, go about the everyday business of making a living.  While economic issues have concerned us for millennia, the field of economics is as exciting and controversial today as it has ever been. Evolving forms of economic activity have brought constant change, making it crucial for modern-day economists to understand and explain the varieties of economic life across communities and nations, and the forces transforming economies over time.  

 

Studying economics allows students to learn about important problems, to understand challenges that the future holds and those overcome in the past, to analyze events whose consequences influence the well-being of millions, and of each one of us individually.  As economist John Maynard Keynes said, studying economics gives the student "a rare combination of gifts": learning how to contemplate the present and future in light of the past, thinking in the abstract and understanding the concrete, understanding the world of business, finance, work, and government policies from the standpoint of broader social concerns.  The skills and knowledge acquired by students majoring in economics make them strong candidates for a broad array of professional careers in the private and public sector.  They also prepare students for admission in graduate programs in economics, law, public policy, business administration, and many others.
summary: This is the official page of the Hofstra Economics Department.

name: Hofstra Math Club
description:
Our meetings include:
- Math seminars and lectures conducted by student members
- Sessions on how to get in solved in research at Hofstra and/or at an REU (Research Experience for Undergraduates), along with tips for taking the GRE, applying to Graduate School, and applying to jobs
- Social meetings to connect with fellow Hofstra students who you may see in upcoming math classes

We look forward to seeing you at future meetings and please feel free to contact us with any questions!
summary: The Hofstra Math Club is here to unify students in various area of STEM under a common foundation: math! Our members come from a plethora of disciplines, and we are here to help them develop friendships through mathematics.

name: Hofstra Neuroscience Club
description: The Hofstra Neuroscience Club brings together students and faculty with a passion for neuroscience. We participate in fundraisers, visit museums, host guest speakers, conferences, engage in community service, and more. Additionally, we collaborate with the Zucker School of Medicine at Hofstra/Northwell once a semester to participate in a neuroanatomy lab with doctors and scientific researchers. HNC welcomes students of all majors with an interest in science and a desire to learn more about the organ that makes you, you!
summary: Hofstra Neuroscience Club is designed to educate, enlighten, and help the community. We abide by our core standards – known as the three C’s – which are the following: Communication, Collaboration, and Creativity.

name: Sitare
description:
Hofstra's Sitare is a South Asian dance team representing diversity and culture in the Hofstra community. Students of all backgrounds are welcome and experience is not necessary! Sitare performs at events hosted by the South Asian Students Association (SASA), such as Diwali and Holi, as well as regional dance competitions across Long Island!. But members are free to simply come to practices and learn the choreographies if they don't want to perform! **THIS IS AN UNDERGRAD ORGANIZATION ONLY**

summary: The Biology Club is dedicated to connecting students with an interest in Biology, through community service, leadership, mentorship, education, and developing relationships between students and faculty.

name: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority Incorporated
description:
Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Incorporated. is a private, not-for-profit organization whose purpose is to provide assistance and support through established programs in local communities throughout the world. The sorority currently has 1,000 collegiate and alumnae chapters located in the United States, Canada, Japan (Tokyo and Okinawa), Germany, the Virgin Islands, Bermuda, the Bahamas, Jamaica, West Africa and the Republic of Korea.

More than 10,000 members typically attend Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Incorporated’s biennial national conventions, and each of the seven regional conferences (held during years when there is no national convention) typically hosts thousands of members. During the Centennial Celebration, the 51st National Convention, held in the District of Columbia, more than 38,000 members registered and attended.
summary: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Incorporated is an organization of college educated women committed to the constructive development of its members and to public service with a primary focus on the Black community.

name: Hofstra Asian Pacific Islander Association
description:
This organization exists to educate the Hofstra community about Asian/Pacific Islander cultures, establish a social network to strengthen the Asian/Pacific Islander American (APIA) community, empower its members by inspiring a passion for advocacy, develop leadership, promote civic engagement, encourage academic excellence, enrich diversity on Hofstra’s campus, and strive to transcend social borders and to foster relations with other groups to create a more unified campus.

This organization is open to all students without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, national or ethnic origin, physical or mental disability, marital or veteran status or any other characteristic protected by state or federal laws.
summary: An organization where the Asian, Pacific Islander, and AAPI ally communities of Hofstra are able to assemble and congregate to learn and be exposed to cultural aspects from the multitude of API heritages.

name: Hofstra Hedge Fund Association
description: Our mission is to cultivate a diverse business community at Hofstra University by empowering students to achieve their academic and professional goals. Meetings consist of a general market discussion followed by informative sessions on various career paths in finance, technical concepts and monthly networking sessions with finance professionals. All majors are welcome!
summary: Our mission is to cultivate a diverse business community at Hofstra University by empowering students to achieve their academic and professional goals through discussions of current events and various educational and career building themes.

name: Phi Beta Kappa
description:
Phi Beta Kappa, the nation’s oldest honor society, was founded in 1776 during the American Revolution by five students at the College of William and Mary. Affiliate chapters were soon founded at Harvard and at Yale, and from there the Society gradually spread, knitting the best minds of the young republic into a community of American scholars. Hofstra University joined this community in 1973, when, after a careful examination of Hofstra’s curriculum and ideals, a group of professors were granted a charter to form the Omega of New York Chapter. Our chapter is now one of 286 in the nation.

Each Spring, the resident members of the Hofstra Chapter meet to induct into the Society a small number of juniors and seniors who are majoring in the liberal arts and sciences. In addition, the chapter sponsors an annual Fall Book Award Luncheon and has hosted visits to campus by some of the nation’s most distinguished scholars.
summary: Phi Beta Kappa, the nation's oldest honor society, was founded in 1776. Hofstra University joined the community of this organization in 1973, after professors were granted a charter to form the Omega of New York Chapter.

name: Project Sunshine
description: Project Sunshine is a national organization with chapters across the country, and the globe! Our chapter is open to anyone in any year and any major. We give Hofstra students the chance to give back to the community, to create fun and memorable moments with hospitalized pediatric patients. We hold events at NYU Winthrop Hospital with their Child Life Program. There, we play and interact with kids by engaging them in games, arts & crafts, etc. Our activities ease some of the stress pediatric patients face in the hospital.
summary: Project Sunshine is a club open to anyone in any year and any major. It gives Hofstra students the chance to give back to the community and to create fun and memorable moments with the children in hospitals.

name: New York State Band Directors Association
description: The New York State Band Directors Association Collegiate Chapter (NYSBDA) is a not-for-profit collegiate organization composed of future Band Directors and Teachers. This shall be accomplished through a program of appropriately designed forums, planned and regulated in a thoroughly democratic manner by the Executive Board and the entire membership in large.
summary: The New York State Band Directors Association Collegiate Chapter is a not-for-profit collegiate organization composed of future Band Directors and Teachers. It is founded for the purpose of improving and promoting instrumental music.

name: Overbooked
description: Overbooked is a book club that is open to all Hofstra undergraduate students. The books to be read are chosen based on the suggestions of club members, which are then submitted to a vote. All genres are included, so as to diversify readings and to delve into as many literary topics as possible. Overbooked promotes and fosters a love for reading within the members!
summary: Overbooked is a book club that is open to all Hofstra undergraduate students. All genres and types of books will be read and chosen monthly by Overbooked members. Overbooked will promote and foster a love for reading within the members.

name: Working Title
description:
Working Title is a writing club open to anyone who wants to further their creative writing skills, whatever type of writing that might be.

Our primary goal in Working Title is to foster a sense of community among fellow writers in order to help members of the club develop their writing in a safe and open environment, one that motivates members to write and share their work with others.

Meetings will be an open-forum setting where writers can come in with creative pieces in any stage of development to discuss with fellow writers. There will also be various free writes and other writing activities.  Topics of discussion will be based on the wants/needs of club members, but will strive to incorporate aspects of different writing mediums and genres (poetry, horror, screenplays, YA, etc.), as well as the development of storytelling skills (character development, world-building, tone, etc.).

The club is open to any undergraduates who currently write, want to write, want to learn to write, and everything in between.
summary: Hofstra's creative writing club for writers of any kind to come together and discuss their creative works or meet other writers.

name: AnimateHofstra
description: The purpose of this organization is to promote animation and motion graphics to the Hofstra community. We seek to teach our members the aspects of animation culture and industry dialogue. The practice of production and technique will be emphasized through discussion and workshops. Screenings of animated works from our own members will also take place for the appreciation of those who have flourished, whether that be to go on into the animation industry or otherwise. Meetings will be held once a week in person. Reminder emails will be sent out weekly from our email: AnimateHofstra@gmail.com!
summary: The purpose of this organization is to cultivate animation at Hofstra. We seek to incorporate aspects of animation culture and industry at our meetings. The practice of production and technique will be emphasized through discussion and workshops.

name: Hofstra International Business Association
description:
The Hofstra International Business Association is a place where students can find a cooperative international environment where they can have the opportunity to connect with people who share an interest in international business.

Students are stimulated to develop their knowledge regarding global cultures and different business practices in these cultures. HIBA hosts meetings every two weeks for a total of seven meeting a semester, where each meeting focuses on an aspect of International Business. We host meetings that have a social, business, and cultural focus, in the effort to create an environment where students can learn and grow from interacting with each other and by educating them with professional material.
summary: The Hofstra International Business Association provides a cooperative and stimulating environment that unites members of the Hofstra community who share an interest in international business and want to have intercultural experiences.

name: Pre-Law (Advising)
description:
Pre-Law Advising at Hofstra University

Services Provided by the Center for University Advising:

The Center for University Advising advises Hofstra undergraduate students and alumni interested in pursuing a career in law. These are just some of the things we can help you with:

  • Selecting courses relevant to the law profession
  • Preparing for the Law School Admissions Test (LSAT)
  • Researching and applying to law school
  • Writing personal statements
  • Providing information on clubs and organizations and other resources 

Students are encouraged to make an appointment with the pre-law advisor, Assistant Dean Kristen Klein, to discuss their interest and plans for pursuing law school. Current Hofstra students may schedule an appointment through the Student Success Connect/Navigate app on the My Hofstra portal.

Hofstra University alumni may call our front desk at 516-463-6770 for appointments or email Dean Klein directly at Kristen.L.Klein@hofstra.edu.
summary: Pre-Law advising through the Center for University Advising

name: Hofstra Nari
shortName: “Nari” means woman in the language Sanskrit
description:
“Nari” means women in the language Sanskrit. This organization is to provide a safe haven for South Asian women on campus. The South Asian community needs to amplify the voices of South Asian women. In a culture that is constantly belittling and speaking down on the needs of South Asian women, we need a platform and a safe space to amplify those voices. Our main goals of the club are empowerment, awareness, community, intersectionality, and diversity of South Asian women.

This program is open to all students without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, national or ethnic origin, physical or mental disability, marital or veteran status or any other characteristic protected by state or federal laws.
summary: This organization is to provide a safe haven for South Asian women on campus. The South Asian community needs to amplify the voices of South Asian women. Our main goals of the club are empowerment, awareness, community, intersectionality and diversity.

name: Disability Rights Education Activism and Mentoring - Hofstra
description:
This club is for any undergraduate student interested in advocating for student rights, increased accessibility, social and policy changes, and aims to provide support and mentorship for disabled students. As an advocacy organization, DREAM works to educate and unify the Hofstra community through disability-centered workshops, events, and collaborations with other clubs and organizations.

This organization is open to all students without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, national or ethnic origin, physical or mental disability, marital or veteran status or any other characteristic protected by state or federal laws.
summary: DREAM Hofstra advocates for student rights, increased accessibility, social and policy changes, and aims to provide support and mentorship for disabled students
