description:
The Center for University Advising offers multiple ways for you to engage with advisors, whether it's through  in-person or Zoom appointments, drop-in Quick Question hours, registration events, group advising sessions and more, we're dedicated to your academic success! 

 

We work in partnership with faculty advisors. Once you declare a major, you will be assigned a faculty advisor within your academic department and should contact them for major-related advising concerns.

 

You can schedule a meeting with your assigned advising dean via NAVIGATE. You can access Navigate through the Hofstra Portal or on your phone. The app NAVIGATE 360 is free on the App Store and Google Play! 

Learn how to schedule an appointment here!
summary: The Center for University Advising (CUA) partners with you to register for courses, understand your degree audit,  plan out your academic journey here at Hofstra and answer questions about academic policies and procedures.

name: Hofstra Student Nurses Association
description: A local chapter of the National Student Nurses Association, come join us to learn more about the nursing profession, interact with the community, and attend events to expose you to the many pathways that becoming a nurse can lead you! We hold Lunch and Learns, fundraising and networking events, stress busters, and professionalism exercises to help guide you towards success, in addition to providing you with resources and opportunities to help you to become the best nurse that you can be!
summary: The purpose of this organization is to enhance the image of nursing in our community by providing activities and outreach events that contribute fundamentally and professionally to nursing education, and to promote cohesion within the nursing community.

name: Middle Eastern North African Student Association
description: MENASA represents the Arabs, Amazeigh, Berbers, Persians, Kurdish, and other ethnic groups. The countries within these ethnic groups are Algeria, Afghanistan, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Bahrain, Comoros Islands, Djibouti, Egypt, Iran, Iraq, Jordan, Kuwait, Lebanon, Libya, Morocco, Mauritania, Oman, Palestine, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Sudan, Syria, Tunisia,Turkey, United Arab Emirates, Yemen.
summary: Our mission is to create a platform where Middle Easterns and North Africans can connect and find people in the Hofstra community sharing the same ethnic background; a space to celebrate our identities, and the similarities and differences among them.

name: Hofstra Comics and Manga Club
description: This club is about comics and manga! The history, the making, and their influence in the modern-day landscape! We're a fun group of people and have been SGA (Student Government Association) recognized since 11/18/22, and we hold events to also entertain fans of the artform which comics and manga are dedicated to. We also do occasional trips to places near campus to check out recent releases in circulation!
summary: The Hofstra Comics and Manga Club (H.C.M.C) was founded to teach and entertain students in the creation of comic book and manga characters, and to also discuss their widespread influence in media within movies, television shows, and popular culture.

name: Jazz Education Network
description:
Join our Group Me: https://groupme.com/join_group/92025245/JFUzD6t

The Jazz Education Network (JEN) is a global non-profit 501(c)(3) organization based in the United States of America that seeks to advance jazz education, promote performance, and develop new audiences. Our chapter of JEN at Hofstra University helps cultivate, foster and grow a culture and community around the great American art form of Jazz. Some of the chapter's events and activities include hosting jam sessions, trips to concerts and jazz museums, holding listening sessions, providing master classes and clinics, attending conferences, and inviting guest artists to perform with our students. Jazz is a very inclusive and democratic style of music and encourages self expression, improvisation, collaboration, and giving a voice to the voiceless. Whether you are passionate about music education, looking for a casual and creative environment to play music, or just a lover of jazz, come join the Jazz Education Network on our musical journey!
summary: The Jazz Education Network at Hofstra University helps cultivate, foster and grow a culture and community around the great American art form of Jazz.  JEN hosts various events including jam sessions, trips to concerts, and workshops.

name: Office of Commuting Student Services and Community Outreach
description: The Office of Commuting Student Services and Community Outreach assists the wide range of students who live off campus, both in the areas immediately surrounding the campus, as well as on and around Long Island. The office assists students in finding a place to live, being safe off campus, and other general tips and tricks to being a commuter student. At the same time, the office works to provide community service opportunities to Hofstra students, including annual programming that occurs both on and off campus, as well as other service opportunities that occur throughout the year.
summary: Office of Commuting Student Services and Community Outreach

name: Intercultural Engagement and Inclusion
description: The programming of Intercultural Engagement and Inclusion (IEI) aims to work closely with students, faculty, administration, and staff to develop programs and initiatives that enable the campus community to explore identity, to enhance intercultural competence, and to develop skills to build an inclusive campus climate. Coordinating cultural-themed heritage months for the University, the IEI staff is responsible for planning and implementing cultural awareness and diversity education programming at Hofstra. In addition, the staff creates programming focused on LGBTQ+ awareness and advocacy. The IEI staff works closely with student cultural clubs and organizations, which provide students with opportunities to explore their cultural identity and find community through peer support, while also learning about other cultures. The staff welcomes everyone, no matter how they identify, to come to the office to explore diversity.
summary: Intercultural Engagement and Inclusion aims to develop programs that enables the campus community to develop skills to build an inclusive campus climate.

name: Orientation & New Student Programs
description:
Hello!

Your Hofstra journey begins with New Student Orientation – an on-campus, three-day, two-night program where all incoming first-year students get their first glimpse of life at Hofstra. Note: Transfer students are offered a one-day orientation experience and should visit the Transfer Orientation webpage for more information about their specific program.

Experience suggests that students who actively participate in an orientation program excel academically and socially more often than those who do not take advantage of such programs. Our orientation program will prepare you for success at Hofstra by introducing you to Hofstra’s curriculum and campus resources; providing opportunities for you to engage with other new students, as well as Hofstra faculty, staff, and orientation leaders; and answering any questions you may have. First-year students also register for fall classes at New Student Orientation. Attendance at orientation is required.

Online registration for New Student Orientation will begin in mid-January through the Hofstra portal. You will need your Hofstra ID and password to sign up. Since dates fill quickly, you must choose three sessions that work for you. Once we receive your request, we will send an email confirmation to your Hofstra Pride email account; the confirmation email will include important information and instructions on taking your foreign language and mathematics assessments online prior to orientation.

We look forward to welcoming you to the Hofstra Pride!

 

Want to be on the Orientation Staff? Find more information about joining the 2026 Orientation Staff here - https://www.hofstra.edu/orientation-new-student/orientationstaff.html
summary: Your Hofstra journey begins with New Student Orientation – an on-campus, three-day, two-night program where all incoming first-year students get their first glimpse of life at Hofstra. Note: Transfer students are offered a one-day orientation experience

Whether you're a seasoned player looking for a competitive outlet or a newcomer eager to learn and improve, our team provides a supportive environment where everyone can thrive. By fostering a culture of teamwork, respect, and continuous improvement, we strive to create memorable experiences and lasting friendships while representing Hofstra University with pride

name: Bengali Student Association
description:
The purpose of the Bengali Student Association at Hofstra is to build a sense of community among Bangladeshi/Bengali students on campus. We also want to bring light to the beautiful culture, while teaching students about the history of Bangladesh’s land and people. Bangladesh has numerous types of cultures, traditions, and 39 spoken languages throughout the country. This club will embrace each cultural background of the Bangladeshi students and bring them together, by celebrating various holidays and significant events at Hofstra University. We hope to expose the community to an interesting and new culture through our traditions.

summary: To build a sense of community among Bangladeshi/Bengali students on campus while teaching students about the history of Bangladesh’s land and people.

name: School of Health Sciences
description: Become a clinical practitioner in your field of interest without having to go medical school with Hofstra University’s licensure-qualifying, career-focused programs. Located in Long Island, New York, the School of Health Professions and Human Services works with local hospital systems like Northwell Hospital to provide students with professional connections and field-related internships. Courses are taught by expert faculty who prioritize one-on-one mentoring and intimate class sizes so that you get the most out of your learning experience. You’ll be able to apply theory and practice through local professional experiences and fieldwork that are integrated into your curriculum. With a degree from Hofstra, you’ll be ready to serve and advocate for your community as a well-rounded and culturally competent healthcare practitioner.

name: Community Engagement and Outreach
description:
As a member of the Hofstra community, you can reach beyond the boundaries of our campus and make a lasting impact in our local and global communities. Hofstra is a proud member of the Uniondale and Village of Hempstead communities, frequently partnering with organizations and government officials to support residents. We invite you to take advantage of Hofstra’s community partnerships and service opportunities so that you can be engaged and support a thriving a community. You can influence the world around you by helping younger students with their homework, by helping Hofstra’s neighbors with their yard work, by being participating in healthy activities for community members, and especially by encouraging everyone to register to vote.

This page provides you with resources and activities to help you Get Involved at Hofstra University and know the neighborhoods around you.

Where are we located? Hofstra University borders The Village of Hempstead and Uniondale, a hamlet within the Town of Hempstead. The University is within Nassau County in the state of New York.

How do I register to vote? Visit Nassau County Board of Elections for information about voting in New York. For a list of candidates in the 2023 elections, 2023-Candidate-List-08282023 (nassaucountyny.gov). (Subject to change.)

Where’s the nearby library? Nassau County Library System Nassau Library System – Strengthening Nassau's Public Libraries Uniondale library Hempstead library
summary: Community Engagement and Outreach

name: Fraternity and Sorority Life at Hofstra
description:
Fraternity and sorority life has been a significant part of the undergraduate experience at Hofstra University since its founding in 1935. The University's continuing commitment to the fraternity and sorority community stems from the significant opportunities the organizations provide for intellectual, cultural, and interpersonal growth.

Each organization was founded on similar principles of scholarship, leadership, service, philanthropy, and belonging. These values are at the core of everything the Hofstra fraternity and sorority community members strive for and integrate into their everyday lives. Hofstra believes that a community with these shared values can foster growth in character and enrich a student's experience through leadership roles, academic achievements, and alumni networking and support.

Please contact greeklife@hofstra.edu for any questions.

description:
Delta Delta Epsilon (DDE) is an international forensic science honors society designed to help further the advancement and development in the field. In the Beta Zeta Chapter, members, who are either forensic science majors or minors, will have the opportunity to develop their forensic science skills and build professional relationships with practicing scientists in the field and with the forensics community within Hofstra.  

Delta Delta Epsilon was founded in 2010 at the University of Central Oklahoma by the Council of Forensic Science Educators. DDE was designed and dedicated to stimulating academic achievement, promoting community understanding, and advancing the fields of forensic science.
summary: Delta Delta Epsilon (DDE) is an international forensic science honors society designed to help further the advancement and development in the field.

name: Hofstra Athletics
description:
The mission of the Hofstra University Department of Intercollegiate Athletics is to sponsor a broad-based athletics program, which positively reflects the interests and values of the institution's students, faculty, administration, alumni, and friends who support its many initiatives.  

The primary objective of the athletic program is to provide student-athletes with the opportunity to excel in the academic environment and in athletic competition.  By recruiting men and women of academic quality, good character, and high athletic ability, the athletic program fosters among its students a sense of leadership, discipline, mutual trust, and social responsibility.  

The athletic program maintains a strong commitment to the principles of fiscal responsibility, gender equity, minority equity, sportsmanship and adheres to the highest standards of ethics and integrity.  Hofstra University is committed to equitable opportunities for all students and its employees, including women and minorities.

Central to its mission, the athletic program directs its efforts in accordance with University, Coastal Athletic Association, and NCAA policies.  Operating in a manner, which reflects positively on Hofstra University, the athletic program aspires to provide the necessary resources for its student-athletes to compete at the highest possible level and it looks favorably upon the opportunity to serve a leadership role in intercollegiate athletics - locally, regionally and nationally.
summary: The Hofstra Department of Athletics sponsors 21 NCAA Division I athletic teams that compete in the Coastal Athletic Association and the Eastern Intercollegiate Wrestling Association.

name: Hofstra Hillel: The Center For Jewish Life on Campus
description: The Center for Jewish Life on Campus is a dynamic community and one of the most active organizations at Hofstra University. We take great pride in students' social, spiritual, and intellectual growth during their time at Hofstra. Through countless events during the year, students have ample opportunities to explore Judaism and connect with friendly and diverse people. Our student leaders and staff take great pride in providing different pathways for students to connect to Jewish life. Come see what we have to offer!
summary: The Center for Jewish Life on Campus is a dynamic community and one of the most active organizations at Hofstra. Our students and staff take great pride in providing different pathways to connect to Jewish life. Come see what we have to offer!

name: Hofstra Cultural Center
description:
The Hofstra Cultural Center (HCC) is an internationally renowned organization that includes a conference and symposium component, performance arts, and the publication of the proceedings of its conferences and symposia. The goal of the HCC is to augment the offerings of the academic departments of the University by developing educational programs related to the cultural and interdisciplinary experience of students, faculty, staff, alumni and scholars.

The HCC plans and coordinates conferences and symposia in the fields of the humanities, business, law and the sciences to promote the University as an international arena of scholarly thought and to foster Long Island as a cultural entity. It has sponsored more than 130 conferences/symposia and has won international recognition for its Women Writers' Conferences and for its Presidential Conference Series, which started in 1982 with the Franklin D. Roosevelt Centennial Conference and continued thereafter. Conferences/symposia have also been held on popular culture personalities such as Babe Ruth, Frank Sinatra, Bing Crosby, Louis Armstrong, James Bond and the New York Mets, to name a few, in addition to conferences on George Sand, John Steinbeck, Don Quixote, Primo Levi, Jean Cocteau, Margaret Thatcher, Oscar Wilde and F. Scott Fitzgerald.

In addition to the conference and symposium component, the Hofstra Cultural Center is dedicated to making Hofstra University a thriving center of the arts (music, theater, dance performances, lectures), by introducing the Joseph G. Astman Cultural Events – which offer opportunities for us to explore who we are as a diverse culture – and by producing cultural events and promoting the arts through grants, services, advocacy, and educational development programs.

Our core values state:

  • The HCC is dedicated to making itself a thriving center of arts activity with relevance to the Hofstra community, visitors and the larger arts community.
  • The HCC believes the arts play an important role in the educational development of our students and that it is not limited to the formal classroom.
  • The HCC serves the Hofstra faculty and student body through its grants and services, advocacy, and cultural programming.
summary: The Hofstra Cultural Center (HCC) is an internationally renowned organization that includes a conference and symposium component, performance arts, and the publication of the proceedings of its conferences and symposia.

name: Student Wellness Education
description:
The Office of Student Wellness Education strive to continuously improve student well-being through SAMHSA’s 8 Dimension of Wellness, which addresses students’ emotional, spiritual, intellectual, physical, environmental, financial, occupational, and social dimensions, to elevate student success in academics and in their future beyond Hofstra University. Check out our website! Link: https://www.hofstra.edu/student-wellness-education/

Want to collaborate with us? Please fill out this form: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSc6I48TORbiaedD2vi77K97TPaiUJf3vKx9ZwifuR0bIEBVKQ/viewform

Interested in learning more about becoming a Peer Wellness Educator? Email us at swe@hofstra.edu!

Looking for help to complete Alcohol Wise, or any 3rd Mill course? Reach out to help@3rdmil.com and/or us!
summary: Student Wellness

name: Clay Club
description: Clay Club is a creative, inclusive environment that allows students of all levels of experience and majors to engage with clay. At organization meetings, members will create their own clay creations inspired by demonstrations or prompts from more advanced members of the club. Members will be able to keep all of their creations and decorate them as they see fit. If funded, we will also take a trip once a semester to a local ceramics studio for a workshop where members will have the opportunity to work on the pottery wheel.
summary: Clay Club is a creative, inclusive environment that allows students of all levels of experience and majors to engage with clay and socialize while creating their own clay creations!

name: AZURE K-pop Cover Dance Group
description:
• We are a Korean Pop dance club that strives to connect people who have an interest in K-pop and dancing. While there are many dance clubs on campus, this one allows students to engage in combined interests. The choreographies created to K-Pop songs are a very important aspect to the genre. Many people who enjoy the genre do so even more with the addition of the choreographies, one of the main selling points of its attraction and very recent explosive interest. Our club, through learning the choreography, coming together to finalize and develop dance formations, and finally filming said choreographies to upload on social media, emulate some of the experience of KPOP idols and the many cover groups internationally that share their love for the genre.
  • Besides dance, we also hold general member events which bring K-pop fans together through cultural and fun activities such as charm making, karaoke, photocard trading, and our annual K-pop party!
summary: AZURE aims to explore and expand knowledge of the performance aspect of the K-pop industry and how multicultural influences have shaped its music. We are a performance based club with a concentration on Korean Pop dance, culture, music, fashion, etc!

name: Women In Music Hofstra
description: 1. Women in Music Hofstra is an organization with a mission to advance the awareness, equity, diversity, heritage, opportunities, and cultural aspects of women in the musical arts through education, support, empowerment, and recognition. It shall be the goal of Women in Music Hofstra to cultivate a community of like-minded individuals who want to advocate for, uplift, and collaborate with other students.
summary: Women in Music Hofstra is an organization with a mission to advance the awareness, equity, diversity, heritage, opportunities, and cultural aspects of women in the musical arts through community building events.

name: Students Supporting Israel
summary: Our mission is to be a clear and confident pro-Israel voice on college campuses, and to support students in grassroots pro-Israel advocacy. This can be done through education, various fun events, peaceful demonstrations as well as tabling.

name: Indo-Caribbean Student Association
description:
The Indo-Caribbean Student Association aims to provide an inclusive space for students from the Caribbean of Indian descent. There is a disconnect between Indian culture and the distinct one created in the Caribbean and this club aims to bridge that gap.

This organization is open to all students without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, national or ethnic origin, physical or mental disability, marital or veteran status or any other characteristic protected by state or federal laws.
summary: The Indo-Caribbean Student Association aims to provide an inclusive space for students from the Caribbean of Indian descent. There is a disconnect between Indian culture and the distinct one created in the Caribbean and this club aims to bridge that gap.

name: Student Voices for Palestine
description: Our club is founded on our support for the people of Palestine to live freely with dignity in their own homes. We are dedicated to speaking out on behalf of Palestinians who are going through unrelenting violence, famine, and starvation. Our mission is to educate others on the matter, raise awareness, and foster civic engagement. We aim to provide a safe space for all those affected by the devastation in Gaza and the West Bank. We are working to bring attention to the ongoing violence, to advocate for, and to celebrate the history of the Palestinian people.
summary: Our club is founded on our support for the people of Palestine to live freely with dignity in their own homes. We are working to bring attention to the ongoing violence, to advocate for, and to celebrate the history of the Palestinian people.

name: Hindu Student Association
description:
The Hindu Students Association aims to serve the Hindu community on campus by facilitating outlets for Hindu students to practice their faith and Hindu based events during their time on campus. 

N. Campus Prayer Space is: Student Center Room 201

S. Campus Prayer Spaces are:

Roosevelt Hall Room 205

Weller Hall 032 (Men’s) and 033 (Women’s)

*HSA membership is for undergraduate students only 

Please contact an Executive Board member to suggest/request an event
summary: Our club aims to spread and highlight the ideas of Hinduism to build a sense of community on campus. Teaching the Hindu values as well as emphasizing the importance behind them is a goal

name: Office of Graduate Admission
description: Earn the professional recognition and personal achievement you seek with a graduate or doctoral degree from Hofstra University. Study online or on campus, full or part-time, with access to hundreds of cooperative, fieldwork, and intern opportunities in the New York area. You’ll learn from award-winning, nationally recognized faculty and a community of peers all contributing to a transformative learning experience. Connect with our team to learn more.
summary: Hofstra University offers more than 175 graduate programs in business, communications, education, engineering, nursing, psychology, and health. We understand how important your professional and personal commitments are to you.

name: Sports Management Association
description: Our purpose is to assist students who are interested in a career in the sports industry and provide exposure for upcoming sports professionals. Students will benefit highly from this club due to the industry driven workshops and industry oriented activities to help kick-start their career.
summary: A way to get aspiring sports professionals integrated into the industry with first-hand exposure to events and workshops that will help drive career growth.

name: Office for Alumni Affairs
description: The Office for Alumni Affairs provides many opportunities for Hofstra graduates and friends for the University to get involved and make a difference – through volunteerism and philanthropy. We also can help alumni and friends stay in touch with each other.
summary: The Office for Alumni Affairs provides many opportunities for Hofstra graduates and friends for the University to get involved and make a difference.

name: The Society of Healthcare Technology Analytics and Innovation for Population Health
description: The Society aims to unite graduate students interested in the ethical application of AI and healthcare informatics to tackle Population health challenges. The organization pursues objectives such as promoting interdisciplinary research, providing career readiness through technical skill development, fostering professional networking, and facilitating discussions on ethical AI use. The society also offers hands-on AI experiences and engages the community through podcasts, social media, and events focused on AI applications for social good. Membership is open to all interested graduate students at Hofstra University, and the organization is governed by an Executive Board.
summary: HTAI is a graduate student organization at Hofstra University focused on fostering interdisciplinary collaboration, enhancing career readiness, and promoting ethical AI use in healthcare.

name: Catholic Campus Ministry
description: Catholic Campus Ministry is dedicated to the holistic enrichment of its students through spirituality, service, and fellowship. Students have the opportunity to learn about and live out their faith in an open environment among their peers. All students are welcome to participate in our regular programs consisting of retreats, international and local mission trips, volunteer opportunities, Bible study, prayer groups, and social activities. Catholic Mass is celebrated on the lower level of the Mack Student Center in the Greenhouse every Sunday at 5pm and every Wednesday at 1:15pm. All members of the Hofstra community are welcome to worship.
summary: The Catholic Campus Ministry at Hofstra supports a community of faith on campus based on the traditions and values of the Roman Catholic Church.

name: Hofstra History Department
description: The Department of History at Hofstra University offers a wide variety of courses on the history of the United States, Europe, Africa, Asia, Latin America, and the Middle East, from ancient and medieval times to the 21st century.
summary: Visit the History Department for engaging events, including guest lectures, socials, and Phi Alpha Theta activities.

name: Friends of Medecines Sans Frontieres
description:
As a student-led organization, Friends of MSF at Hofstra is dedicated to supporting and promoting the mission of Médecins Sans Frontières/Doctors Without Borders (MSF). We work to raise awareness on campus about global health inequities, humanitarian crises, and the lifesaving work MSF provides in more than 70 countries. Through advocacy, education, fundraising, and community engagement, we help amplify MSF’s commitment to delivering emergency medical care to people affected by violence, disease, malnutrition, exclusion from health care, and natural or human-made disasters.

By fostering an informed and compassionate student community, we stand in solidarity with MSF’s mission to go where others can’t or won’t, because that’s where help is needed most.

“Right now, Doctors Without Borders emergency medical teams—made up of over 30,000 doctors, nurses, and logistics experts—are treating patients in more than 70 countries. These teams save the lives of those threatened by violence, disease, malnutrition, exclusion from health care, and catastrophic events. They go where many other organizations can’t or won’t because that’s where they’re needed most.”
summary: These teams of doctors save the lives of those threatened by violence, disease, malnutrition, exclusion from health care, and catastrophic events. They go where many other organizations can’t or won’t because that’s where they’re needed most.

name: Project EmpowHer
description: The EmpowHer Club is a student-led organization dedicated to empowering women of all backgrounds through support, education, and advocacy. Our club aims to create a safe and inclusive space where women can come together to address issues such as gender inequality, domestic violence, and systemic barriers to empowerment. Through workshops, events, and community outreach, we strive to provide resources and support to women in need while also fostering a sense of solidarity and sisterhood among members.
summary: Project EmpowHer is a club that aims to advocate for underserved communities of women such as those who are homeless, domestic abuse victims, refugees etc.

name: Women in Film Hofstra
description: Women in Film at Hofstra aims to provide a safe space for aspiring women filmmakers to connect, collaborate, and create. We strive to establish a vibrant community where members can uplift each other's voices. Women in Film at Hofstra intends to foster an inclusive environment through education and empowerment to highlight women’s place in the industry. Some of our future meeting plans include holding screenings, workshops, and possibly pannels.
summary: Women in Film Hofstra is for all students interested in learning more about women filmmakers. We aim to foster an inclusive environment through education and empowerment, highlighting women’s place in the film industry.

name: The Shutter Wing Club
description:
Do you want to...
-Learn more about photography?
-Take pictures at events for various clubs and departments on campus?
-Build a photography portfolio?
-Create content with fellow photographers?
Then join The Shutter Wing at Hofstra University! No experience or camera equipment necessary!
summary: Shutter Wing is a club for all majors who want to learn more about photography. No experience or camera equipment needed! Message us on our Instagram if you're interested in collaborating!

name: Student Physical Therapy Club
description:
The purpose of the club will be to increase
social awareness and promote advocacy in the field, in particular related to physical
activity and collaboration in the health field.
summary: The organization's purpose will be to promote collaboration, friendship, and professional development amongst members, promote understanding, and create opportunities in physical therapy.

name: Alpha Psi Omega
description: The Mu Psi cast of Alpha Psi Omega dedicates themselves to supporting theater in all forms at Hofstra University.
summary: National Theatre Honor Society - Our aim is to encourage dramatic production at every step in a person’s academic career.

name: Office of Study Abroad Programs
description:
Studying abroad is a life-changing experience that fosters your personal, academic, and professional growth. When you study abroad you become a part of a global community, all while earning credits towards your degree. 

The Office of Study Abroad Programs provides Hofstra students with these global opportunities. Want to spend a semester taking course in Spain or Japan? Maybe you want to take a January course in the UK or do a summer internship in Italy? We will help you find the program that fits your goals and guide you through the study abroad process. Start exploring your options today!
summary: Your Hofstra journey can be a global journey by participating in a study abroad program. The Office of Study Abroad Programs provides Hofstra students with these global opportunities.

name: Office of Equity and Inclusion
description: The Office of Equity and Inclusion (OEI) works to drive an equitable and inclusive culture for students, faculty, and staff. We aim to educate, advocate, engage, and empower the Hofstra community by cultivating partnerships and resources to create and sustain a diverse culture of belonging. Leveraging diversity across all spectrums is how we all achieve success.
summary: The Office of Equity and Inclusion endorses a variety of campus-wide events and programs throughout the year, all aimed at celebrating the diverse community and promoting multicultural understanding.

name: Chavaya
description:
Chavaya at Hofstra University ✡️
💫 Inspiring the next generation of Jewish leaders
📖 Educating, empowering & enlightening 
🤝 Join a community that values growth & meaningful connection
📚 Learn, lead, and make an impact!
summary:
Chavaya at Hofstra University ✡️
💫 Inspiring the next generation of Jewish leaders
📖 Educating, empowering, & creating a vibrant future
🤗 Join our community of growth & meaningful connection
📚 Learn, lead, and make an impact!

name: Black Leaders Advocating for Change
summary: Black Leaders Advocating for Change promotes the creation of an inclusive and equitable environment tailored to the needs of students, faculty, and staff in the Hofstra community who identify as Black, Indigenous, and/or People of Color (BIPOC).

name: Dark Room
description:
Darkroom is a professional development organization for students of color in the RTVF fields. We facilitate workshops with students, educate them about internships as well as helping students build portfolios and improve professional skills in RTVF.

This organization is open to all students without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, national or ethnic origin, physical or mental disability, marital or veteran status or any other characteristic protected by state or federal laws.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to fulfill the need for students of color to be involved in organizations, events, or projects in the School of Communications.  We strive to curate a space where inclusivity and diversity thrive.

name: Hofstra Black Nurses Organization
description:
The Hofstra Black Nurses Association (HBNA) is a student-led organization dedicated to empowering and supporting Black nursing students at Hofstra University. Our mission is to foster academic excellence, provide mentorship, and create networking opportunities that prepare members for success in the healthcare field.

We advocate for diversity, equity, and inclusion in nursing while addressing health disparities affecting Black communities. Through professional development, community service, and outreach initiatives, we strive to uplift and inspire future Black nurses. HBNA serves as a safe space for students to connect, share experiences, and grow together as leaders in healthcare.

This organization is open to all students without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, national or ethnic origin, physical or mental disability, marital or veteran status or any other characteristic protected by state or federal laws.
summary: The Hofstra Black Nurses Association (HBNA) empowers Black nursing students through mentorship, professional development, and community service. We foster academic excellence, advocate for health equity, and create a supportive network for future nurses.

name: Leading Ladies
description:
Leading Ladies strives to serve as an outlet for creative expression and alliance for women and minorities involved in theatre at Hofstra University. We intend to foster a safe space for women to gather and collaborate on theatrical events and activities. Examples of how we plan to carry this out include feminist play readings and discussions, staged readings of original women students’ works, genderbent cabarets or productions, workshops for helping women find audition material, creating a festival of women’s theatrical works, and hosting guest speakers from successful women in the theatre industry. With this club, we hope to raise awareness of the double standard in the performance and production industry, and set women up with connections to help them succeed. 

This organization is open to all students without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, national or ethnic origin, physical or mental disability, marital or veteran status or any other characteristic protected by state or federal laws.
summary: Leading Ladies strives to serve as an outlet for creative expression and alliance for women and minorities involved in theatre at Hofstra University. We intend to foster a safe space for women to gather and collaborate on theatrical events and activities

name: Medical Ethics and Dilemmas Society
description:
Welcome to Medical Ethics and Dilemmas Society (MEDS)! The mission of the Medical Ethics and Dilemmas Society (MEDS) is to foster a community of students dedicated to exploring and understanding the complex ethical issues in medicine and healthcare. MEDS aims to promote thoughtful discussion, critical analysis , and collaborative problem-solving to address ethical dilemmas, both historical and contemporary. The society is committed to cultivating ethical leadership, empathy, and a commitment to finding morally sound alternatives to questionable medical practices, preparing its members to navigate the challenges of the medical field with integrity and compassion. 

 

Being a member of MEDS offers a unique opportunity for students interested in medicine and healthcare to explore the ethical side of the field. MEDS provides a space to discuss and analyze complex moral issues, such as end-of-life care and the ethics of new medical technologies. These discussions help members develop critical thinking skills and a deeper understanding of the ethical challenges they may face in their future careers. 

 

MEDS also brings together students from different backgrounds, creating a diverse and collaborative environment. This diversity enriches conversations and helps members see ethical dilemmas from various perspectives, fostering empathy and a more comprehensive approach to medical ethics. Being part of MEDS is not just about gaining knowledge — it’s also about building a network of like-minded peers and mentors who are passionate about ethical leadership in medicine. 

 

In addition to personal growth, MEDS membership looks great on a resume. It shows a commitment to ethical practice and leadership, qualities that medical schools and employers highly value. Not only will members of MEDS be able to gain knowledge from speakers, presentations and events, but members can also take on leadership roles, organize events, and work on research projects, gaining valuable experience and skills along the way. Overall, MEDS prepares students to become thoughtful and ethical leaders in the medical field.
summary: Medical Ethics and Dilemmas Society (MEDS) is a brand new club open to all students that want to learn about navigating the challenges of the medical field. We organize discussions, speakers, presentations and scenarios, all to bring ethics into light.

name: Melanin Movers
description: At Melanin Movers; our mission is to empower and elevate dancers of color by providing a supportive, innovative, and inclusive space to thrive. Through masterclasses, panel discussions, mentorship, field trips, and collaborative opportunities, we are committed to equipping our members with the tools, resources, and connections necessary to excel in their dance careers. We celebrate heritage, creativity, and dedication, fostering a community where talent is nurtured, dreams are realized, and the next generation of dancers takes their artistry to new heights.
summary: Melanin Movers is dedicated to empowering dancers of color by creating a supportive and inclusive space for growth. Through masterclasses, mentorship, and collaborative opportunities, the organization equips members with the resources and connections to

name: Men's Club Volleyball
description: Hofstra University Men’s Volleyball Club is a competitive and inclusive team that welcomes players of all skill levels, from experienced athletes to those looking to develop their game. We provide a supportive environment for students to improve their volleyball skills, compete in tournaments, and build lasting friendships. Our team participates in local and regional competitions, practices regularly, and fosters a strong sense of teamwork and sportsmanship. Whether you're looking to compete or just play for fun, join us to be part of Hofstra’s growing volleyball community!
summary: Hofstra University Men’s Volleyball Club is a competitive and inclusive team for all skill levels. We practice regularly, compete in tournaments, and foster teamwork and sportsmanship. Come be part of our growing volleyball community!

name: The National Society of Leadership and Success
description: The National Society of Leadership and Success is an organization that provides a life-changing leadership program that helps students achieve personal growth, career success, and empowers them to have a positive impact in their community. As the only nationally accredited leadership program, the NSLS' rich curriculum empowers students with experience ranging from entry-level to mastery. Through communication-forward programs, a series of speaker broadcasts, and a tight-knit community of like-minded and supportive leaders, the NSLS gives each student the tools they need for the future of their dreams. Along with the formal education from the international NSLS, we foster in-depth understanding through community and specialized programs. Instead of individuals molding around a program, we strive to include as diverse of an audience ranging from novice leaders to experts. Entirely, we provide a once-in-a-lifetime education that will make your world a better place.
summary: We are a collaborative network of burgeoning or established leaders dedicated to the spread and development of leadership. Through effective external programming, combined with in-depth group discourse, the NSLS creates the next generation of leaders.

name: For the CULTURE
description: 1. The purpose of For the CULTURE shall be to provide artists and music business professionals of color within the Hofstra community with an inclusive space to network, collaborate, and amplify their voices. This includes advocating for diverse representation in the music industry, offering mentorship and career development opportunities, and fostering community engagement through creative expression and professional growth.
summary: For the CULTURE was created by and for artists and music business professionals of color within the Hofstra community to provide an inclusive space to network, collaborate, and amplify their voices.

name: HU Clinical Psychology PHD Equity and Social Justice Initiative
description:
The organization aims to:
1. Promote DEI initiatives within the university’s curriculum and policies.
2. Engage with the community through educational outreach and advocacy.
3. Provide a platform for students to collaborate on initiatives supporting historically
underrepresented and marginalized communities.
4. Offer opportunities for personal and professional growth through events, workshops, and
mentorship programs.
summary: ESJI is dedicated to fostering an inclusive academic and social environment at Hofstra University by addressing and advocating for diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI).

name: First Year Experience
description:
Through three powerful FYE components, you'll build connections, discover your strengths, and feel right at home in the academic community from the very beginning.

FYE Learning Outcomes

  • Adjust confidently to college life with guidance and support from caring faculty.
  • Form connections with your instructor and classmates in a smaller, more personal, setting with students who are going through the same transition as you.
  • Feel a sense of community and belonging as you begin your Hofstra journey.
  • Cultivate your intellectual curiosity by exploring new ideas and questions that matter to you.
  • Build skills in analysis, critical thinking, and problem-solving that will serve you in every major.
  • Strengthen your ability to communicate – whether in writing, speaking, or creative work.
summary: Hofstra's First-Year Experience (FYE) is a cornerstone of the University's commitment to supporting new students, reflecting the goals outlined in Hofstra 100, the University's strategic plan.

name: Collegiate Science & Tech Entry Program
description:
Through a coordination of campus-wide services including counseling, tutoring and mentoring – designated to enrich student life – our goal is student success, where our hallmark is community building. Five categories of services are provided through the CSTEP Program including the Summer Transition Program, Academic Support Services, Student Academic Resources, Student Career Development and Graduate School Preparation.

 

Purpose
To support students interested in pursuing careers in science, math and technology or those that require pre-licensure.

Mission
To provide an environment where students acquire academic and lifelong skills to become outstanding scholars and professionals.

Vision
To provide students with an array of academic opportunities and professional preparedness skills to succeed in their careers.
summary: The Hofstra University Collegiate Science and Technology Entry Program (CSTEP), a New York State Education Department grant, aims to support undergraduate and graduate students interested in science, technology, engineering and mathematics (STEM).

name: Honors Board
description:
• To oversee ongoing efforts to maintain the visibility and effectiveness of Hofstra's Honor Code via marketing campaigns, websites, and other promotional activities. This responsibility entails close coordination with Marketing and Communications, Student Enrollment, Engagement, and Success, and the Student Government Association.
  • To form ad hoc appeals committees to resolve appeals involving academic integrity violations. The ad hoc committee will consist of three voting members chosen from the Honor Board, including one student, one academic administrator, and one faculty member. In addition, the ad hoc appeals committee will contain four nonvoting members including representatives from the Provost's Office, the Division of Student Enrollment, Engagement, and Success, and the Dean's Office where the alleged violation was said to have occurred and the department (normally the department chair) where the alleged violation was said to have occurred.
  • To coordinate the development of instructional and informational resources (including workshops, websites, visiting speakers, etc.) designed to support community members (both faculty and students) in academic integrity.
summary: To oversee ongoing efforts to maintain the visibility and effectiveness of Hofstra's Honor Code via marketing campaigns, websites, and other promotional activities.

name: Creativz Dance Team
summary: Creativz is an intermediate dance team that creates and performs multicultural choreographies for endless events and organizations, on and off Hofstra's campus!

name: Dance Marathon
description:
Dance Marathon is a non-profit program of Children’s Miracle Network Hospitals (CMN Hospitals). CMN Hospitals raise funds for 170 children’s hospitals that support the health of 10 million kids each year across the U.S. and Canada. Donations go to local hospitals to fund critical life-saving treatments and healthcare services, along with innovative research, vital pediatric medical equipment, child life services that put kids’ and families’ minds at ease during difficult hospital stays and financial assistance for families who could not otherwise afford these health services.

Through Dance Marathon, students and campus communities have raised over $300M for local children’s hospitals since 1991. Dance Marathon was brought to Hofstra’s campus in 2017 and has since raised over $100,000. Hofstra’s Dance Marathon supports Cohen’s Children’s Medical Center right here on Long Island. Our program at Hofstra is led by our organization, in collaboration with the student performance club, Danceworks Productions, and pre professional fraternity, Phi Delta Epsilon. Throughout the school year, our organization fundraises, plans and participates in other philanthropies in hopes of bringing the Hofstra community together to support a wonderful cause.
summary: Dance Marathon is a non-profit program of Children’s Miracle Network Hospitals. Our organization raises money for Cohen's Children's Hospital and collaborates with DanceWorks, Phi Delta Epsilon and Phi Kappa Theta during our annual event in the Fall.

name: Eastern European Student Union
description: The purpose of this organization shall be to create a community of people who are either a part of or want to learn about Eastern European culture. Culture is a way to connect with others and learn to appreciate our similarities and differences. It is a club where students with similar backgrounds can find each other and bond over traditions, culture, values, language, music, history, and food. It is a way to foster friendships through socialization, bonding, and learning from each other.
summary: To cultivate an environment for students who share or are interested in learning and participating in Eastern European cultures

name: First-Gen Starting Line
description:
First-Gen Starting Line is a student-led organization dedicated to supporting, celebrating, and uplifting first-generation and Hofstra students as they begin to navigate their higher education journey. We believe that being the first is not a limitation — it’s a legacy in the making.

Our mission is to build a strong, inclusive community where first-gen and non first-gen Hofstra students can connect through shared experiences, gain access to resources, and grow together both personally and academically. We foster a culture of empowerment, where every student at the "starting line" feels seen, supported, and inspired to move forward with confidence.

Through programs such as open dialogue spaces, academic workshops, and community-building events, the First-Gen Starting Line serves as both a launchpad and a landing place — a space where students can find encouragement, celebrate milestones, and build lifelong connections.

Because the journey may start with one — but it’s stronger with many.
summary: First-Gen Starting Line builds community for all Hofstra students through connection, mentorship, and support—empowering students to grow, thrive, and lead together from the very start of their college journey.

name: Hofstra Men in Nursing
description:
Hofstra Men in Nursing (HMN) is a student organization affiliated with the American Association for Men in Nursing (AAMN). Our mission is to highlight the contributions and achievements of male nurses while fostering mentorship opportunities between current male nurses and male nursing students. Recognizing that men are an underrepresented minority in nursing, HMN seeks to empower future nurses by celebrating diversity and breaking down barriers in the profession.

HMN is committed to creating a welcoming and supportive community for all undergraduate students—regardless of gender or major—who are interested in nursing, healthcare, or simply supporting the mission of advancing men in the nursing profession.

Throughout the semester, HMN will host a variety of events designed to encourage academic success, professional growth, and social connection. These include study sessions, guest speaker events, and opportunities to relax and have fun as a group.

By joining HMN, students will gain access to valuable networking opportunities, peer and professional mentorship, and a community dedicated to promoting diversity and inclusivity in the nursing field.
summary: Hofstra Men in Nursing (HMN), a branch of AAMN, celebrates the achievements of male nurses, supports students through mentorship and community, and raises awareness of men as an underrepresented minority in nursing—open to all majors and genders.

name: Engineers in Action
description: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZqDYd4mh6d8
summary: Engineers in Action connects students with communities worldwide to design and build sustainable infrastructure, like pedestrian bridges, that improve access to schools, healthcare, and opportunity while giving members hands-on engineering experience.

name: Pakistani Student Association
summary: Pakistani Student Association is a cultural and social club that celebrates Pakistani heritage through events, food and music. We welcome all students to join, connect, and learn more about Pakistan’s rich culture while building a community.

name: Planned Parenthood Generation Action at Hofstra
description: Planned Parenthood Generation Action at Hofstra empowers students to advance reproductive health, rights, and justice through education, advocacy, and service. In partnership with Planned Parenthood of Greater New York (PPGNY), we host trainings, volunteer opportunities, and civic engagement. Grounded in intersectionality, our programming centers equity and accessibility, builds student leadership, and collaborates with campus/community partners to uplift diverse voices and reduce health disparities. All students are welcome.
summary: PPGA at Hofstra advances sexual health and reproductive rights through education, advocacy, and service. With PPGNY, we host trainings, volunteer work, and civic engagement centered in intersectional equity, access, & leadership. Open to all students!

name: Street Soulz Collective
description:
"Street Soulz Collective" is a Hip-Hop culture club. Our purpose is to gather any and all students who love the culture of hip hop or are interested in learning more. Our club events are themed on the foundational elements of hip hop culture including: Breaking (dance), Graffiti (writing), Deejaying(music), Mcing(speech) and Fashion in a casual, immersive environment. 

The mission of Street Soulz Collective (S.S.C) is to create a positive & inclusive safe space that would work to become an extended family for students to network, unwind, and feel motivated to create with others at Hofstra; that includes high quality activities. We here for all members to feel respected and valued.

Activities include: Workshops from guest speakers/instructors who have been in a part of the industry/hip-hop culture, within their respective elements. Movement classes  (ex: freestyle cyphers, go to party moves, choreography, etc.
summary: A multi-purposed club focused on embracing the foundational elements of Hip Hop culture.

name: Production and Songwriting Association
summary: Production and Songwriting Association is Hofstra’s new hub for producers and songwriters. We focus on hands-on studio projects, feedback sessions, workshops with industry pros, and building a creative community that learns by making music together.

name: Institute of Innovation and Entrepreneurship/ideaHUb
description: The IIE fosters an environment that inspires learning, collaboration, impactful research, and the growth of innovative business ventures.Located in Guthart Hall, Room 103, the ideaHUb—a New York State Certified Business Incubator—offers entrepreneurs and early-stage companies access to invaluable resources, including expert mentorship, access to cutting-edge Maker Space tools (including 3D printers laser cutter, etc.), and resources to help startups and early-stage ventures grow. Whether you’re a student with a big idea or an entrepreneur ready to launch, the ideaHUb is your place to turn vision into reality!
summary: Hofstra University’s Institute of Innovation and Entrepreneurship (IIE) is a hub for bold ideas, collaboration, and real-world impact.

name: NOAH Scholars
description: The NOAH Scholars Program sparks momentum in driven students and propels them into standout academic experiences at Hofstra University, a place where ambition becomes real achievement. Scholars don’t just learn here. They accelerate. They take on challenges that demand focus, creativity, and persistence. They sharpen their voice, expand their abilities, and push past limits that once felt out of reach. This program is designed for students who want to strengthen their communities and create an impact that reaches well beyond campus.
summary: The New Opportunities at Hofstra Program (NOAH) has provided highly motivated and diverse students with access to higher education at one of the country’s finest postsecondary institutions since the program’s inception in 1964.

name: Student Veterans of America, Undergraduate Chapter
description: The Hofstra Student Veterans Association (SVA) is a developing student organization dedicated to supporting and advocating for student veterans on campus. It aims to create a strong community by providing resources, networking opportunities, and peer support to help veterans transition into academic and civilian life. Through collaboration with faculty, student organizations, and diverse student veterans, the SVA seeks to enhance the college experience, promote leadership, and ensure veterans’ voices are heard.
summary: The Hofstra Student Veterans Association (SVA) is a developing undergraduate student organization dedicated to supporting veterans, ROTC cadets, and military-connected students at Hofstra, aiming to foster a strong, unified military-affiliated community.
